- REVIEW 2026-06-23: Reviewed uncommitted CreatePixmap duplicate-id state/resource-model compatibility patch (X11Connection.kt + X11State.kt + XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt). NO BLOCKERS. The new CreatePixmap guard returns BadIDChoice(error=14) with opcode 53 and badValue=id before `state.drawable(drawableId)`, `state.putPixmap`, or `own(id)` (X11Connection.kt:1481-1499), so a duplicate pixmap id cannot replace the existing pixmap and is not added to the rejecting connection's owned-resource set. `state.hasResource(id)` covers the modeled resource namespaces that matter for CreatePixmap uniqueness: windows, pixmaps, GCs, fonts, cursors, colormaps, RENDER pictures, glyph sets, and GLX contexts (X11State.kt:1637-1646); semantic MCP Steroid Find Usages confirmed the current hasResource guards are CreatePixmap and CreateGC only, while putPixmap has only the CreatePixmap caller. Rejecting duplicates across windows/pixmaps/GCs/render pictures/glyph sets/GLX contexts is protocol-compatible because X resource ids are live-resource unique; there is no legitimate CreatePixmap use case that may reuse a live id without FreeResource/client cleanup first. Owned-resource cleanup remains correct: failed duplicate creation returns before `own(id)`, and normal cleanup still removes pixmaps, GCs, fonts, cursors, colormaps, GLX contexts, pictures, and glyph sets by owned id (X11State.kt:1739-1747; X11Connection.kt:2887-2889). The regression test proves the main behavior by creating a 1x1 pixmap, painting (0,0) blue, issuing duplicate CreatePixmap with a different 2x2 size, asserting BadIDChoice, then GetImage-reading the original PixmapId and observing the blue pixel still present (XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt:447-469). Non-blocking test gap: the test proves usability and pixel preservation, but not the original drawable geometry directly; a GetGeometry assertion after the duplicate error could prove width/height remain 1x1. Compatibility caveat outside this patch: other create paths still overwrite existing ids because they do not call hasResource (CreateWindow, RENDER CreatePicture/CreateGlyphSet/ReferenceGlyphSet/CreateSolidFill/CreateLinear/Radial/ConicalGradient, GLX context creation, OpenFont/CreateCursor/CreateColormap), so this patch fixes CreatePixmap only and does not make duplicate-id enforcement uniform. - REVIEW 2026-06-23: Reviewed only test quality for the uncommitted RENDER ReferenceGlyphSet duplicate-resource-id regression in XRenderProtocolTest.kt. NO BLOCKERS. The raw protocol test (XRenderProtocolTest.kt:1593-1622) is hang-safe via `socket.soTimeout = 2_000`, batches all requests (CreateWindow, CreateGlyphSet, ReferenceGlyphSet@new=WindowId/existing=GlyphSetId, CreatePicture on WindowId, FillRectangles green, GetImage) before any read, and bounds shutdown with serverThread.join(1_000). Error assertions match writeError layout (X11Connection.kt:2403-2412): type 0, BadIDChoice/error 14, badValue=WindowId@4, minor opcode 18@8, RENDER major opcode@10; the guard `if (state.hasResource(id)) return writeError(...)` fires before `state.referenceGlyphSet`/`own` (X11Connection.kt:591), and hasResource(WindowId) is true because the window was created (X11State.kt:1637-1638). Request/reply ordering is correct: ReferenceGlyphSet is the only request that yields a reply/error, so the 32-byte error arrives first and the GetImage reply follows (read via readReply at :2130-2134, pixel asserted 0xFF00FF00 at offset 32). The regression DOES fail on the previous missing-error behavior: with no hasResource guard, no error packet is emitted, so readExactly(32) reads the GetImage reply header whose [0]==1, failing `assertEquals(0, duplicateError[0])`. It proves the original WindowId resource remains usable after rejection by creating a picture on it, filling green, and reading the green pixel back. NON-BLOCKING observations: (1) old `referenceGlyphSet` writes only to the glyphSets map and never touches `windows` (X11State.kt:531-534), so the window was never actually clobbered even under old behavior — the green-pixel preservation check alone does NOT discriminate old vs new; the discriminating power is entirely the error-packet assertion + error-before-reply ordering, which is sufficient. (2) The duplicate id chosen is WindowId (a window) rather than a glyph set id; this validly exercises the generic hasResource check and reuses window-pixel readback, but does not separately prove a glyph-set/glyph-set id collision; acceptable since hasResource is type-agnostic. (3) Sequence number (bytes 2-3) is not asserted, consistent with sibling tests. Verification: focused `./gradlew test --tests org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XRenderProtocolTest` BUILD SUCCESSFUL; git diff --check clean. Maintainer/pattern check: surrounding raw-socket RENDER tests and X11Connection handlers are Eugene Petrenko's recent work; style matches the batched-socket + exact-error-packet + framebuffer-assertion idiom and the just-landed CreateGlyphSet/CreatePicture duplicate-id tests. - REVIEW 2026-06-23: Reviewed only test quality for the uncommitted RENDER CreateCursor duplicate-resource-id regression (XRenderProtocolTest.kt:310-338, helper renderCreateCursor :1941-1948). NO BLOCKERS. Hang-safe: socket.soTimeout=2_000 plus serverThread.join(1_000) bound every read and shutdown; all requests (CreateWindow@WindowId, CreatePicture@PictureId drawable=WindowId/Rgb24, CreateCursor cursor=WindowId source=PictureId, FillRectangles@PictureId 1x1 green, GetImage@WindowId 1x1) are batched before any read. Request/reply ordering correct: CreateCursor is the only failing request and FillRectangles is replyless, so the 32-byte error arrives first (readExactly(32)) then the GetImage reply (readReply, pixel@offset32). Cursor body encoding matches spec: 12-byte body cursor@0 + source@4 + x@8 + y@10, request length 4 units. Error assertions match writeError layout (X11Connection.kt:2408-2416): type 0@0, BadIDChoice/error 14@1, badValue=WindowId@4 (= the duplicate cursor id, per guard `badValue=id`), minor opcode 27@8 (= CreateCursor, confirmed via dispatch :260 and helper opcode :1947), RENDER major opcode@10 — consistent with the guard `if (state.hasResource(id)) return writeError(...minorOpcode=27...)` at X11Connection.kt:751 firing before putCursor/own so the rejected request mutates nothing. Empirically VERIFIED the regression catches the prior missing-error behavior: with the guard line disabled the test fails at :326 assertEquals(0, duplicateError[0]) because readExactly(32) then reads the GetImage reply whose [0]==1; restored tree leaves only the intended +1 line. NON-BLOCKING observations: (1) the image-readback green assertion proves WindowId remains usable as a drawable and the connection does not desync after a mid-stream error, but unlike the sibling CreatePicture/SolidFill tests it does NOT independently prove "without replacing", because putCursor (X11State.kt:1729) adds to a separate cursors set and never overwrites the windows map — the error-packet assertion is the sole load-bearing discriminator; (2) sequence number (bytes 2-3) not asserted, consistent with all sibling duplicate-id tests. Verification: ./gradlew test --tests org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XRenderProtocolTest --rerun-tasks BUILD SUCCESSFUL, results 46 tests / 0 failures / 0 skipped with the CreateCursor case present and passing.
- REVIEW 2026-06-23: Reviewed only test quality for the uncommitted RENDER CreateAnimCursor duplicate-resource-id regression (XRenderProtocolTest.kt:340-368, helper renderCreateAnimCursor :1980-1984). NO BLOCKERS. Hang-safe: socket.soTimeout=2_000 plus serverThread.join(1_000) bound every read and shutdown; all requests (CreateWindow@WindowId, CreatePicture@PictureId drawable=WindowId/Rgb24, CreateAnimCursor cursor=WindowId, FillRectangles@PictureId 1x1 green, GetImage@WindowId 1x1) are batched before any read. Request/reply ordering correct: CreateAnimCursor is the only failing request and FillRectangles is replyless, so the 32-byte error arrives first (readExactly(32)) then the GetImage reply (readReply, pixel@offset32). Error assertions match writeError layout (X11Connection.kt:2409-2418): type 0@0, BadIDChoice/error 14@1, badValue=WindowId@4 (= the duplicate cursor id, per guard badValue=id), minor opcode 31@8 (= CreateAnimCursor, confirmed via dispatch :264 and helper opcode :1983), RENDER major opcode@10 — consistent with the new guard `if (state.hasResource(id)) return writeError(...minorOpcode=31...)` at X11Connection.kt:830 firing before putCursor/own so the rejected request mutates nothing. ANSWER Q1 (fails on prior missing-error behavior?): YES — pre-patch renderCreateAnimCursor ran putCursor/own and emitted no error, so readExactly(32) would consume the GetImage reply whose [0]==1, failing assertEquals(0, duplicateError[0]) at :356; the regression genuinely catches the missing error. ANSWER Q2 (proves original resource remains usable?): PARTIALLY — the green readback proves WindowId stays a usable drawable and the connection does not desync after a mid-stream error, but like the sibling CreateCursor test (:310-338) it does NOT independently prove "without replacing": putCursor adds to a separate cursors set and never overwrites the windows map, so even pre-patch the window would still render green; the error-packet assertion is the sole load-bearing discriminator. Body-encoding note (NON-BLOCKING): helper sends a 4-byte body = cid only with zero ANIMCURSORELT entries (real clients send >=1 8-byte element); adequate here because the duplicate-id guard fires on the cid before any element parsing, and the disassembler (:1148) computes elements=0 without crashing. Other NON-BLOCKING nit: sequence number (bytes 2-3) not asserted, consistent with all sibling duplicate-id tests. Verification: ./gradlew test --tests org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XRenderProtocolTest BUILD SUCCESSFUL, CreateAnimCursor case present and passing in results (0 failures); git diff --check clean.

- REVIEW 2026-06-23: Reviewed only test quality for the uncommitted CORE CreateCursor duplicate-resource-id regression (XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt:515-542, helper createCursorRequest :1866-1875). NO BLOCKERS. Q1 (fails on old behavior, passes with guard?): YES — EMPIRICALLY VERIFIED. In a throwaway worktree at HEAD with ONLY the test diff applied (source guard absent), the test fails at :533 assertEquals(0, duplicateError[0]) because old createCursor emits no error, so readExactly(32) consumes the GetImage reply whose [0]==1; the working tree (guard present) passes. Main working tree left untouched (only the two intended modified files remain). Q2 (correct X11 error shape?): YES — assertions match writeError layout (X11Connection.kt:2410-2419): type 0@0, error 14@1 (BadIDChoice — the correct X11 error for an already-in-use resource id), badValue=WindowId@4 (= the duplicate id, per guard `badValue=id`), major opcode 93@10 (CreateCursor). Q3 (proves stream recovery + resource usability?): YES — after the 32-byte error the test reads the GetImage reply (readReply) and asserts pixel(0,0)==0xff0000ff blue, drawn by the replyless PolyPoint to WindowId; this proves the server kept processing requests after a mid-stream error AND that WindowId remains a usable drawable window (not replaced by a cursor) — directly validating "without replacing existing resource". Request/reply ordering correct: CreateCursor is the only failing request and PolyPoint is replyless, so the error arrives first then the single GetImage reply; no stray bytes. Body encoding correct: 28-byte CreateCursor body (cursor@0, source@4, mask@8, fore/back CARD16 colors, x/y), request length 8 units; source=PixmapId / mask=PixmapId+1 are valid depth-1 pixmaps (irrelevant since the hasResource guard fires before reading them, but non-confounding). Guard ordering verified: `if (state.hasResource(id)) return writeError(...)` at X11Connection.kt:2112 fires before putCursor/own so the rejected request mutates nothing. Structure mirrors the sibling CreatePixmap(opcode 53):463-474 and CreateWindow(opcode 1):481-508 duplicate-id tests; assertion shape identical. NON-BLOCKING nits: (1) the duplicate id reuses WindowId (a window) rather than first creating a cursor then colliding — valid because hasResource is type-agnostic and this cross-type collision is arguably stronger (a cursor-create cannot clobber a window), but there is no test where the pre-existing resource is itself a core cursor; optional enhancement only. (2) sequence number (bytes 2-3) and minor opcode (bytes 8-9, default 0) not asserted — consistent with ALL sibling duplicate-id tests. Verification: focused ./gradlew test passed with the CreateCursor case present (58 tests, 0 failures, 0 skipped); old-behavior failure reproduced in isolated worktree then worktree removed; main git status shows only the intended X11Connection.kt + XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt changes.

NO BLOCKERS. This change resolves the earlier-logged BLOCKER (opcode hardcoded to 93 for both paths) by threading the real major opcode into the shared handler; the CreateGlyphCursor test's `assertEquals(94, duplicateError[10] & 0xff)` is the load-bearing assertion that discriminates the fixed code from that buggy-intermediate version. CHECK 3 (helpers): both encode 28-byte spec-shaped bodies (cursor@0, source/sourceFont@4, mask/maskFont@8, CARD16 colors, x/y), request length 8 units; the guard only needs body.size>=4 and id@0, both satisfied. NON-BLOCKING observations: (1) both tests force the collision by reusing WindowId (a window) rather than first creating a cursor then colliding — valid because hasResource is type-agnostic and a cross-type collision is arguably stronger (a cursor-create cannot clobber a window); no test exercises a cursor-vs-cursor id collision (optional enhancement only). (2) CreateGlyphCursor passes sourceFont/maskFont=GcId (a GC, not a font) — harmless because the guard fires on id@0 before any source/mask is read. (3) sequence number (bytes 2-3) and minor opcode (bytes 8-9) not asserted — consistent with all sibling duplicate-id tests. - REVIEW 2026-06-23: Independent TEST-COVERAGE review of the uncommitted GLX CreateNewContext duplicate-resource-id change (X11Connection.kt:1036 guard `if (state.hasResource(context)) return writeError(error=14, opcode=XGlx.MajorOpcode, minorOpcode=XGlx.CreateNewContext, badValue=context)` before putGlxContext/own; new test XGlxProtocolTest.kt:91-110, helper createNewContextBody:163-169). ONE BLOCKER (confirms prior reviewer's finding, reached independently). BLOCKER: the test's stated third goal — "proves the rejected duplicate did not replace the original context by checking IsDirect remains 0 after a duplicate direct=true request" — is NOT actually proven. glxIsDirect (X11Connection.kt:1085-1090) hardcodes `reply[8] = 0` and never consults state.glxContext(context)?.direct, so `assertEquals(0, direct[8].toInt())` at :108 is vacuous: it holds whether or not the guard exists, even if the duplicate direct=true HAD replaced the original. There is currently no protocol-observable surface that distinguishes a replaced context from the original (IsDirect is hardcoded; MakeContextCurrent returns the same id either way; renderType/direct are not queryable), so non-replacement cannot be asserted as written. The error-packet assertion is the sole load-bearing discriminator. NON-BLOCKING (the checks that DO pass): CHECK 1 (fails-old/passes-new): PASSES with the guard (focused ./gradlew test EXIT=0). Old-behavior failure is deterministic by construction (verified against source, no tree mutation): pre-guard the duplicate CreateNewContext emits no reply (success is replyless), so the first readExactly(32) consumes the IsDirect reply whose type byte[0]==1, failing `assertEquals(0, duplicateError[0])` at :101. CHECK 2 (error shape): type 0@byte0, error 14 (BadIDChoice)@byte1, badValue==contextId@bytes4-7 via u32le (little-endian, client byte-order 0x6c), minor opcode 24 (CreateNewContext)@bytes8-9 via u16le, GLX major opcode 128@byte10 — all match writeError layout (X11Connection.kt:2413-2422). PASS. CHECK 4 (helper spec-shape): createNewContextBody encodes a 24-byte body = context@0 + fbconfig(RootFbConfigId)@4 + screen@8 + render_type(RgbaType)@12 + share_list@16 + is_direct+3pad@20, matching the GLX CreateNewContext (opcode 24) wire format and the handler's offsets (context@0/fbConfig@4/screen@8/renderType@12/direct@body[20]); identical to the inline body in the passing lifecycle test :62-72 with direct parameterized. PASS. hasResource is type-agnostic and includes glxContexts (X11State.kt:1646), so the first CreateNewContext registers the id and the second collides correctly. RECOMMENDATION: either drop the "without replacing" claim from the test name/intent (the change is adequately guarded by the error assertion alone), or make replacement observable (e.g. have glxIsDirect return the stored context's direct flag, then create the original with direct=false and assert IsDirect still reports 0 / fails if replaced by direct=true). NON-BLOCKING nits: sequence number (bytes2-3) not asserted, consistent with all sibling duplicate-id tests; socket.soTimeout=2_000 + serverThread.join(1_000) keep the test hang-safe. Verification: ./gradlew test --tests org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XGlxProtocolTest EXIT=0 (guarded tree); review-only, no files edited/committed/pushed.
- REVIEW 2026-06-23: Independent test-coverage review of the uncommitted GLX CreateNewContext duplicate-resource-id guard (X11Connection.kt:1036 + IsDirect stored-state read at :1089; test XGlxProtocolTest.kt:91-111, helper :163-170). NO BLOCKERS. All four task checks confirmed: (1) Test fails on old behavior — pre-change CreateNewContext emitted no error, so readExactly(32) would consume the IsDirect reply whose [0]==1, failing assertEquals(0, duplicateError[0]) (reply[0]=1 vs error[0]=0 verified at X11Connection.kt:2383/2416). (2) Error assertions match writeError layout (X11Connection.kt:2414-2423): type 0@0, error 11 (BadAlloc)@1, badValue=duplicate contextId@4, minor opcode 24 (CreateNewContext)@8, GLX major opcode 128@10. (3) Non-replacement proof is sound: indirect created first (direct=false), duplicate direct=true rejected, IsDirect returns 0 because reply[8] now reads state.glxContext(context)?.direct (X11Connection.kt:1089) — had the duplicate replaced the stored context, IsDirect would read direct=true→1 and fail. (4) Helper createNewContextBody encodes spec-shaped 24-byte body: context@0, fbconfig@4, screen@8, render_type@12, share_list@16, is_direct@20(+3 pad) — matches GLX opcode-24 wire format and the production body[20] read. Request/reply ordering correct: only the duplicate (error, 32B) and IsDirect (reply, 32B) produce output; soTimeout=2_000 keeps it hang-safe. Verification: ./gradlew test --tests org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XGlxProtocolTest --rerun-tasks → tests=4 failures=0. NON-BLOCKING concern: error code 11 (BadAlloc) diverges from all 13 sibling duplicate-id guards in this file, which use error 14 (BadIDChoice) for already-in-use client XIDs (X11Connection.kt:345,583,751,1163,1432,1498,...) — X spec says an in-use resource id → BadIDChoice. The test faithfully pins 11 (matches impl + task spec), so coverage is correct, but the implementation's chosen code is inconsistent and may warrant a follow-up; if changed to 14, this test's assertEquals(11,...) must update in lockstep. NON-BLOCKING nit: the IsDirect==0 assertion only functions as a non-replacement proof because the IsDirect-stored-state change ships in the same diff; with the guard alone (IsDirect still hardcoded 0) it would pass trivially — the two changes are correctly coupled here.

- REVIEW 2026-06-23: Reviewed current uncommitted core SetInputFocus implementation; NO BLOCKERS. Opcode 42 now dispatches to setInputFocus, reads focus at request body offset 0 while leaving time at offset 4 unused, takes revert-to from the request data byte, rejects short bodies with BadLength, invalid revert-to values with BadValue, and invalid non-special focus ids with BadWindow. - REVIEW 2026-06-24: Reviewed current uncommitted access-control state + semantic snapshot exposure (X11State.kt, X11Connection.kt, XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt); NO BLOCKERS. Thread safety OK: the five new accessors and snapshot() are @Synchronized on the X11State instance lock (X11State.kt:262-281, 489-490) and snapshot reads accessControlEnabled + accessHosts.toList() under that lock (X11State.kt:595-598), so the snapshot is atomic. Value equality OK: XAccessHost(family:Int, address:List<Int>) data class (X11State.kt:2307) gives structural List equality, so the LinkedHashSet dedups correctly; duplicate insert is a no-op preserving order and delete of an absent host is a no-op (X11State.kt:274-281) = X spec. accessControl in XScreenSnapshot is consistent with the semantic layer (sits beside screenSaver/pointerControl/requestCounts; single construction site X11State.kt:532). Wire format conforms: ChangeHosts family@0/len@2/addr@4+pad (X11Connection.kt:2790-2796), ListHosts reply byte1=enabled, count@8, 22 unused, HOST{family,len,addr,pad} (X11Connection.kt:2808-2821), SetAccessControl empty body mode 0/1. Residual risks (non-blocking): (1) list is recorded but never ENFORCED — no connection accept/deny path references accessHosts (by design for an observational server, but default-permissive is total, not just the default flag); (2) error-precedence inconsistency — changeHosts checks mode(BadValue) before length(BadLength) at X11Connection.kt:2789-2790 while sibling setAccessControl/forceScreenSaver check length first; (3) ServerInterpreted validation is custom (requires non-empty type + non-empty value split by one NUL, 7-bit ASCII; X11Connection.kt:3659-3663) and badValue uses address.size/family which the protocol leaves implementation-defined; the test only proves self-consistency for these. Verification: ./gradlew test --tests org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XCoreDrawingProtocolTest BUILD SUCCESSFUL; new round-trip test passes.
- REVIEW 2026-06-24: Reviewed current uncommitted CirculateWindow implementation/test coverage (X11Connection.kt, X11State.kt, XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt); NO CODE BLOCKERS. The new protocol test pins QueryTree bottom-to-top order before/after RaiseLowest and LowerHighest (XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt:3045-3049), and the local spec confirms QueryTree lists children in bottom-to-top stacking order, so this is a meaningful model-stack assertion. It also validates full CirculateNotify event bytes including type=26, zero detail, sequence, event/window ids, place, and padding (XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt:3046,3048,4960-4969), plus BadValue/BadLength/BadWindow sequence numbers for invalid direction, wrong body length, and missing window (XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt:3050-3052). Residual non-blocking coverage gaps: (1) there is no true no-op circulate case where no mapped child is occluded/occluding, so an implementation that still restacks in all-disjoint or all-unmapped cases would not be caught; (2) the third disjoint child proves a non-overlapping sibling survives the successful restacks, but does not prove non-overlap alone suppresses CirculateNotify; (3) SVG/HTTP render order is not directly asserted after circulation, even though SvgScreenRenderer draws snapshot windows in stackingIndex order derived from X11State.windows.values (SvgScreenRenderer.kt:228-260, X11State.kt:524-540), so a future renderer traversal/sort regression could pass the new protocol test. IDE inspections on scoped files reported only existing style-level warnings/patterns (not changed CirculateWindow logic); Find Usages confirmed opcode 13 -> connection handler -> X11State.circulateWindow -> sendCirculateNotify. Verification: git diff --check clean; MCP Steroid buildAllModules errors=false aborted=false; focused CirculateWindow test passed; full XCoreDrawingProtocolTest passed (108 tests, 0 failures). Maintainer/pattern: blame around touched protocol dispatch/events/state/tests is Eugene Petrenko; eventual commit should use recent short imperative style, e.g. "Handle core CirculateWindow requests". Process note: this is one independent review; project quorum still requires a second independent review for the non-trivial multi-line patch if applying the full Stage 9 gate.
- REVIEW 2026-06-24: Reviewed current uncommitted core CirculateWindow implementation (X11Connection.kt, X11State.kt, XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt); NO BLOCKERS. Opcode 13 dispatch uses request data byte as direction and a fixed 4-byte WINDOW body, matching the spec encoding "1 opcode, 1 direction, 2 request length, 4 WINDOW window" with request length 2 units (X11Connection.kt:122,1303-1311; test helper XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt:4163-4167). Validation order is length first (BadLength/error 16), then direction values 0 RaiseLowest / 1 LowerHighest (BadValue/error 2 with badValue=direction), then missing window (BadWindow/error 3 with badValue=window id), all before state mutation; the new test asserts these packets and stream recovery (XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt:3035-3053). State ordering is protocol-aligned for QueryTree bottom-to-top lists: childrenOf filters insertion/restack order, RaiseLowest picks the first mapped child with an overlapping mapped sibling above it and moves it to the top, LowerHighest scans from top and moves the first overlapping occluder to the bottom (X11State.kt:173-192,2166-2199); the test observes [first,second,third] -> [second,third,first] -> [first,second,third] through QueryTree. CirculateNotify layout matches the spec encoding for event code 26: byte0=26, byte1 unused, sequence@2, event window@4, restacked window@8, unused@12, place byte@16, remaining unused (X11Connection.kt:2962-2969; XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt:4960-4968). Residual non-blocking compatibility gaps: no SubstructureRedirect/CirculateRequest path and event delivery remains the existing project simplification of writing structure events directly on the requesting connection rather than filtering by StructureNotify/SubstructureNotify selections; no exposure processing for formerly obscured siblings. Verification: MCP Steroid scoped inspections reported only existing style warnings away from changed CirculateWindow lines; semantic Find Usages showed state.circulateWindow is only called by the opcode 13 handler; MCP Steroid buildAllModules errors=false aborted=false; git diff --check clean; focused ./gradlew test --tests org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XCoreDrawingProtocolTest --rerun-tasks BUILD SUCCESSFUL. Quorum note: three CirculateWindow review run-agent processes (run_20260623-231801-2810/2811/2812) still had pid.txt and empty stdout at review time, so this entry is one completed review pending comparison with those agents when they finish.
- REVIEW 2026-06-24: Reviewed current uncommitted core UngrabPointer/UngrabKeyboard + active input-grab snapshot change (X11Connection.kt, X11State.kt, SvgScreenRenderer.kt, XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt). ONE BLOCKER logged to ISSUES.md. Opcode 27/32 dispatch and handlers are wired, use fixed 4-byte timestamp bodies, return no reply on success, and return BadLength(16) with stream recovery for invalid sizes. Explicit ungrab only clears the active grab owned by the current connection via owner identity. State snapshots expose active grabs in /state.json as inputGrabs, and window/client-disconnect cleanup clears matching grabs. BLOCKER: FreeCursor/opcode 95 goes through closeResource -> X11State.removeResource, which removes the cursor id but does not call releaseInputGrabsForResources; a pointer grab using that cursor remains in inputGrabs with a stale cursor after resource cleanup, violating the task requirement that client/resource cleanup removes stale active grabs. - REVIEW 2026-06-24: Reviewed current uncommitted core UngrabPointer/UngrabKeyboard + inputGrabs state exposure (SvgScreenRenderer.kt, X11Connection.kt, X11State.kt, XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt). BLOCKER logged to ISSUES.md: explicit FreeCursor dispatches through closeResource -> X11State.removeResource(id), which removes the cursor but does not call releaseInputGrabsForResources, so a pointer grab recorded with cursor C survives FreeCursor C and /state.json can expose stale cursor "0x..." in inputGrabs. Protocol handlers for opcodes 27 and 32 otherwise use fixed 4-byte timestamp bodies, return BadLength on non-4-byte bodies, and are replyless on success (next QueryPointer reply proves no ungrab reply). Owner-scoped explicit ungrab is implemented by comparing active grab owner to the current X11Connection. Snapshot JSON adds inputGrabs after inputOperations with pointer/keyboard entries and nullable confineTo/cursor fields. Non-blocking test gaps: tests cover replyless success, short BadLength recovery, and minimal JSON visibility, but do not cover oversized ungrab bodies, non-owner ungrab preserving another client's active grab, client disconnect cleanup, DestroyWindow cleanup, or FreeCursor cleanup; the missing FreeCursor case is currently an implementation blocker. Verification: git diff --check clean before logging; MCP Steroid inspections on changed Kotlin files reported only existing style/duplication warnings away from new ungrab code; MCP Steroid Find Usages confirmed new state methods are reached from X11Connection and cleanup paths; MCP Steroid buildAllModules errors=false aborted=false; ./gradlew test --tests org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XCoreDrawingProtocolTest --rerun-tasks passed (116 tests, 0 failures). - REVIEW 2026-06-24: Re-reviewed current uncommitted core UngrabPointer/UngrabKeyboard + inputGrabs cleanup after the FreeCursor fix. NO BLOCKERS. The prior FreeCursor stale-cursor blocker is RESOLVED in the current diff: FreeCursor opcode 95 still dispatches through closeResource -> X11State.removeResource(id), and removeResource now calls releaseInputGrabsForResources(setOf(id)) after deleting the resource (X11Connection.kt:200,1637-1642; X11State.kt:2049-2063). That helper clears an active pointer grab when its grab window, confineTo, or cursor id is in the removed-resource set (X11State.kt:335-349), so a pointer grab using a freed cursor no longer remains visible in /state.json. The new regression test creates a cursor, grabs the pointer with cursor C, verifies inputGrabs[].cursor, sends FreeCursor C, and asserts inputGrabs is empty while stream framing recovers through QueryPointer (XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt:2589-2618). Opcode 27/32 dispatch and handlers use exact 4-byte timestamp bodies and return BadLength(16) on invalid sizes; success remains replyless because ungrabPointer/ungrabKeyboard only mutate state and write no reply, and the replyless tests prove the next packet read is the following QueryPointer reply (X11Connection.kt:135-142,1560-1563,1592-1595; tests :2477-2527,:2647-2699). Explicit ungrab remains owner-scoped by comparing the active grab owner to the requesting X11Connection. Snapshot JSON appends inputGrabs with nullable confineTo/cursor and unsigned time. Verification: git diff --check clean; focused ./gradlew test --tests org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XCoreDrawingProtocolTest --rerun-tasks passed; full ./gradlew test passed (199 tests, 1 skipped, 0 failures/errors); MCP Steroid scoped inspections reported only existing style/duplication warnings away from changed lines; MCP Steroid semantic ReferencesSearch confirmed new state APIs are only reached from dispatch/cleanup paths; MCP Steroid buildAllModules errors=false aborted=false. Acceptable to commit.
- REVIEW 2026-06-24: Reviewed current uncommitted core GrabServer/UngrabServer implementation (opcodes 36/37). NO BLOCKERS. Dispatch now routes 36/37 to handlers that accept only empty bodies and return BadLength(16) with opcode 36/37 for non-empty bodies; success writes no reply. The request loop waits on server-grab release after reading the body but before sequence increment, request recording, and dispatch, so non-owner X11 requests do not advance sequence or execute while another connection owns the grab; the owner bypasses the wait and can continue issuing requests. X11State tracks owner identity under a ReentrantLock/Condition, releases on explicit owner UngrabServer and on connection cleanup, and /state.json appends serverGrabbed. Tests cover replyless owner progress, BadLength stream recovery, non-owner blocking until UngrabServer, release on owner disconnect, and serverGrabbed JSON visibility. Residual non-blocking gap: tests do not queue multiple non-owner requests behind a grab to prove post-ungrab stream recovery beyond the first blocked request, but the loop structure should handle it. Verification: git diff --check clean; MCP Steroid buildAllModules errors=false aborted=false; MCP Steroid scoped inspections reported only existing style/duplication warnings away from changed lines; MCP Steroid ReferencesSearch showed new state methods are only reached from request-loop/dispatch/cleanup/snapshot paths; focused XCoreDrawingProtocolTest passed; full cleanTest test --rerun-tasks passed (205 tests, 1 skipped, 0 failures/errors). Maintainer/pattern: blame around touched protocol/state/test areas is Eugene Petrenko; recent commit subjects use short imperative form, so "Handle core server grab requests" is format-compliant. Acceptable to commit.
- REVIEW 2026-06-24: Review of uncommitted CORE opcode 36 GrabServer / 37 UngrabServer slice (X11Connection.kt dispatch 36->grabServer(body)/37->ungrabServer(body) at :147-148, handlers :1599-1607, await at run-loop :80, release in finally :89; X11State.kt serverGrabLock/Condition/serverGrabOwner :58-60, awaitServerGrabRelease/grabServer/ungrabServer/releaseServerGrab/serverGrabbed :365-400, disconnect cleanup releaseServerGrab(sink) :450, snapshot serverGrabbed=serverGrabbed() :706 + XScreenSnapshot field :2896; SvgScreenRenderer.kt /state.json "serverGrabbed" :161; tests XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt :2785-2904 + helpers/waitForStateContains). NO BLOCKERS, acceptable to commit. Semantics correct: empty-body GrabServer/UngrabServer succeed replyless; non-empty body -> BadLength(16) with major opcode 36/37 (sequence still increments, stream recovers); non-owner request processing parks in awaitServerGrabRelease before sequence/dispatch; grab owner proceeds (while-condition excludes owner==self); grab released on explicit UngrabServer and on owner disconnect. CONCURRENCY/DEADLOCK: clean. serverGrabLock is a leaf; awaitServerGrabRelease holds it only while await() (which releases it) and never holds the X11State intrinsic monitor; the only nesting is intrinsic->serverGrabLock inside @Synchronized snapshot() reading serverGrabbed(), so no lock-order inversion. while-loop guards spurious wakeups; signalAll on release; ReentrantLock+Condition justified over wait/notify. Owner identity is referential (X11Connection is a plain class, no equals override) so owner matching is correct. CLEANUP: finally calls releaseServerGrab(this) before removeClientResources/unregister, and removeClientResources also calls releaseServerGrab(sink) — idempotent, no double-free risk. SNAPSHOT FIDELITY: serverGrabbed read under serverGrabLock; JSON `],"serverGrabbed":<bool>}` is valid; tests assert true/false transitions. NON-BLOCKING: (1) ungrabServer() and releaseServerGrab() are byte-identical — collapse to one. (2) New client connections still complete setup() during a grab (await is only in the post-setup request loop); real X11 also defers new connection processing under a server grab — minor fidelity gap, low priority. (3) A hung grab owner that never disconnects freezes all other clients indefinitely (no await timeout) — this matches real X11 GrabServer semantics, noted not flagged. VERIFICATION: ./gradlew test XCoreDrawingProtocolTest --rerun-tasks BUILD SUCCESSFUL, 4 new grab testcases all pass (delays-test=0.305s confirms the 300ms park actually blocks the other client until UngrabServer). Commit-message convention: prior commits use imperative "Handle core <X> requests"; suggest "Handle core GrabServer and UngrabServer requests".
- REVIEW 2026-06-24: Reviewed current uncommitted core GrabServer/UngrabServer slice (opcodes 36/37). BLOCKER logged to ISSUES.md. The empty-body handlers correctly return BadLength(16) with major opcode 36/37 for non-empty bodies and write no reply on success; /state.json exposes serverGrabbed; explicit owner UngrabServer and owner disconnect release the state. However, the server-grab gate is not atomic with non-owner sequence/dispatch: X11Connection.run calls state.awaitServerGrabRelease(this) at :80, releases serverGrabLock, then increments sequence/records/dispatches at :81-86, while another thread can set serverGrabOwner in X11State.grabServer at :373-376 between those steps. That permits a non-owner request that passed the gate just before GrabServer became active to dispatch after the server is grabbed, violating the task requirement that other client processing pause before sequence/dispatch until UngrabServer/disconnect. Existing tests wait for serverGrabbed before sending the other client's request, so they do not cover this race. Verification: git diff --check clean before logging; MCP Steroid inspections on touched files found only existing style/duplication warnings outside changed lines; MCP Steroid ReferencesSearch confirmed new grab methods are wired from request loop/dispatch/cleanup/snapshot; MCP Steroid buildAllModules returned errors=false aborted=false; IDE Gradle test run exited 1 before publishing SM test nodes once, CLI fallback initially hit an intermittent Gradle EOFException, then rerun `./gradlew test --tests org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XCoreDrawingProtocolTest --rerun-tasks --console=plain --stacktrace` passed with BUILD SUCCESSFUL and the XML report showed 125 tests, 0 failures/errors, including all four new GrabServer cases. Maintainer/pattern: blame around touched protocol/state/test areas is Eugene Petrenko; recent commit subjects use short imperative form, so "Handle core server grab requests" is format-compliant after the blocker is fixed. Not acceptable to commit yet.
- REVIEW 2026-06-24: Re-reviewed current uncommitted core GrabServer/UngrabServer slice after the atomicity blocker fix. NO BLOCKERS. The prior wait/check blocker is RESOLVED: X11Connection now wraps sequence increment, trace logging, request recording, and dispatch in X11State.processWhenServerGrabAllows at X11Connection.kt:80-87, and X11State.processWhenServerGrabAllows holds requestProcessingLock while checking serverGrabOwner and while running the protected processing block at X11State.kt:366-388. Therefore a non-owner cannot pass the gate and then dispatch after another client makes GrabServer visible; any owner GrabServer must first acquire the same processing lock. Opcode 36/37 dispatch remains correct at X11Connection.kt:148-149, handlers require empty bodies and return BadLength(16) for non-empty bodies at X11Connection.kt:1600-1607, and successful requests remain replyless. Cleanup releases the grab on connection close at X11Connection.kt:90 and X11State.kt:461-467; /state.json exposes serverGrabbed through X11State.kt:723 and SvgScreenRenderer.kt:161. The requested regression coverage is present: XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt:2841-2872 queues two non-owner QueryPointer requests behind an active GrabServer, proves no reply during the grab, then verifies both replies arrive after UngrabServer with sequence numbers 1 and 2. Verification: git diff --check clean; MCP Steroid scoped inspections on changed Kotlin files reported only existing style/duplication warnings away from the new server-grab logic; MCP Steroid ReferencesSearch showed server-grab APIs only reached from request loop/handlers/cleanup/snapshot-render paths; MCP Steroid buildAllModules errors=false aborted=false; `./gradlew test --tests '*GrabServer*' --rerun-tasks --console=plain --stacktrace` passed; full `./gradlew test --rerun-tasks --console=plain` passed with 205 tests, 1 skipped, 0 failures/errors. One direct class-filter Gradle attempt (`--tests org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XCoreDrawingProtocolTest`) failed during JUnit discovery with NoClassDefFoundError for XServer, but the wildcard focused run and full suite passed. Maintainer/pattern: blame around adjacent request-loop/state/test code is Eugene Petrenko; recent commits use short imperative subjects such as "Handle core input ungrab requests", so "Handle core server grab requests" is format-compliant. Acceptable to commit.
- REVIEW 2026-06-24: Reviewed current uncommitted core AllowEvents slice (opcode 35). NO BLOCKERS. Dispatch now routes opcode 35 to allowEvents, uses the request data byte as an unsigned mode, requires the fixed 4-byte TIMESTAMP body, returns BadLength(16) with opcode 35 for non-4-byte bodies, and returns BadValue(2) with badValue=mode for modes outside 0..7. Valid requests are replyless and record semantic intent only via X11State.recordInputControlOperation; /state.json exposes inputControlOperations with operation, numeric mode, modeName, and unsigned timestamp, which is coherent and honest for this slice because it does not pretend to implement synchronous replay/freeze behavior. Tests cover replyless success, BadValue, BadLength, stream recovery through a following QueryPointer, no state recording after invalid requests, and JSON state including unsigned timestamp. Non-blocking coverage gap: the BadLength test uses a zero-length body but not an oversized body; implementation checks body.size != 4, so oversized requests should follow the same path. Verification: git diff --check clean; MCP Steroid scoped inspections reported only existing style/duplication warnings away from new AllowEvents lines; MCP Steroid ReferencesSearch found allowEvents called from dispatch, recordInputControlOperation called from allowEvents, and XInputControlOperation used from state/snapshot JSON paths; MCP Steroid buildAllModules errors=false aborted=false; focused `./gradlew test --tests '*AllowEvents*' --rerun-tasks --console=plain` passed with 2 tests; full `./gradlew test --rerun-tasks --console=plain` passed with 207 tests, 1 skipped, 0 failures/errors. Maintainer/pattern: blame around adjacent core input protocol/state/test code is Eugene Petrenko; recent subjects use short imperative "Handle core ... requests", so "Handle core AllowEvents requests" is format-compliant. Acceptable to commit.
- REVIEW 2026-06-24: Reviewed current uncommitted core ChangeActivePointerGrab + pointer-event mask validation slice. BLOCKERS logged to ISSUES.md. Good: opcode 30 dispatches to `changeActivePointerGrab`; the wire body is parsed as cursor@0, time@4, eventMask@8 with exact 12-byte BadLength behavior; successful/no-effect requests are replyless; BadCursor and BadValue are emitted for bad cursor ids and invalid POINTEREVENT bits; owner scoping is enforced in X11State.changeActivePointerGrab; updating eventMask/cursor while preserving original grab time is correct; `ValidPointerEventMask=0x7ffc` covers POINTEREVENT bits 2..14 and is used only by GrabPointer/GrabButton/ChangeActivePointerGrab. - REVIEW 2026-06-24: Reviewed uncommitted active-pointer-grab change (ChangeActivePointerGrab, core opcode 30) + /state.json eventMask rendering. Scope: X11State.kt (changeActivePointerGrab :331-336; XInputGrab/XInputGrabSnapshot eventMask field + eventMaskHex :3059/3071/3084/3092), SvgScreenRenderer.kt (:161 JSON), wired via X11Connection.kt (:30->changeActivePointerGrab :1620-1635; eventMask validation added to grabPointer/grabButton/grabKeyboard) and XInputController.kt (ValidPointerEventMask=0x7ffc). NO BLOCKERS. Verified claims: (1) Original grab time PRESERVED — grab.copy(eventMask=,cursor=) leaves time untouched; only event-mask+cursor change, confineTo/modes/ownerEvents/windowId all preserved (matches X11 spec; ChangeActivePointerGrab never updates last-grab-time). (2) Time gating via validUngrabTime(time,grab.time) = requestTime==0||unsigned>=grabTime — honors "no earlier than last-grab time"; consistent with ungrabPointer (server-time upper bound not tracked anywhere in codebase). (3) Owner-only: grab?.owner==owner guards mutation; non-owner & no-grab cases silently ignored (no error), per spec. (4) Wire format correct: request-length 4 => 16B total => 12B body; cursor@0,time@4,event-mask@8; body.size!=12 -> BadLength(16). (5) Validation order cursor(BadCursor 6)->mask(BadValue 2) runs before grab lookup, matching real server request-level checks; ValidPointerEventMask 0x7ffc == PointerGrabMask (bits2..14, ButtonPress..KeymapState). (6) Active-grab cleanup safe: new cursor stored in grab so releaseInputGrabsForResources tracks it; old cursor dropped. (7) JSON valid: eventMaskHex non-null, "eventMask":"0x.." inserted between ownerEvents and pointerMode, comma-correct. Semantically useful for debug. Verification: ./gradlew test --tests *XCoreDrawingProtocolTest* BUILD SUCCESSFUL (4 new ChangeActivePointerGrab tests + full suite pass); compile UP-TO-DATE/clean. Minor non-blocking nits (NOT required): validUngrabTime name is reused for a change (not ungrab) op — purely cosmetic; keyboard grabs now serialize "eventMask":"0x0" in /state.json (harmless, snapshot reuses one data class).

- REVIEW 2026-06-24: Re-reviewed uncommitted ChangeActivePointerGrab change after blocker fixes (X11Connection.kt, X11State.kt, XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt). NO BLOCKERS REMAIN — both 2026-06-24 blockers RESOLVED. BLOCKER 1 (future timestamps mutate active grab): fixed by new `validChangeActivePointerGrabTime` (X11State.kt:406-411) enforcing `CurrentTime || (requestTime >= grabTime && requestTime <= currentServerTime)`, with `currentServerTime(floor)=max(inputTime,floor)` (X11State.kt:413-414); `changeActivePointerGrab` (X11State.kt:331-337) preserves original grab time via copy and is owner-scoped. Matches X11 spec (request ignored if time earlier than last-grab-time or later than current server time). Regression test: grab@time=5, future ChangeActivePointerGrab@time=6 ignored (eventMask stays 0x40, cursor unchanged, replyless); older@4 and equal@5 also covered. BLOCKER 2 (GrabPointer oversized accepted): fixed to `body.size != 20 -> BadLength(16)` (X11Connection.kt:1533) so over/undersized both BadLength before BadValue/BadCursor; test sends 24-byte oversized body -> BadLength opcode 26 seq4 with stream recovery via following QueryPointer. Non-blocking notes: server "time" is a logical counter (`inputTime`, advanced only on button events, X11State.kt:562) not wall-clock, so the future-timestamp upper bound is relative to that model — internally consistent and matches how grab/ungrab times are minted; current-server-time bound was applied only to ChangeActivePointerGrab (the "ideally also grab/ungrab" suggestion left out, acceptable per blocker scope); keyboard grabs serialize "eventMask":"0x0" in /state.json (harmless, shared snapshot class). Verification: git diff --check clean; full rebuild via --rerun-tasks compiles clean (XInputGrab now requires eventMask at all call sites — all updated); 3 new ChangeActivePointerGrab tests pass (XML report tests=3 failures=0 errors=0); full ./gradlew test passed (215 tests, 1 skipped, 0 failures/errors). Acceptable to commit. Maintainer/pattern: adjacent core input protocol/state/test code is Eugene Petrenko; recent commit subjects are short imperative ("Handle core GrabButton and UngrabButton requests") — a matching subject such as "Handle core ChangeActivePointerGrab requests" is format-compliant.
- REVIEW 2026-06-24: Independent REVIEW of uncommitted core X11 KillClient (opcode 113). Files: X11Connection.kt, X11State.kt, XInputController.kt, XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt. VERDICT: NO BLOCKERS — commit-ready as `Handle core KillClient requests`. Correctness vs spec confirmed: dispatch added at X11Connection.kt:230; killClient (X11Connection.kt:3284-3296) validates exact 4-byte body (else BadLength error=16), reads CARD32, treats 0 as AllTemporary (companion const AllTemporary=0 :3296+) -> destroyTemporaryRetainedClients(); for non-zero, BadValue (error=2) on !hasResource; then destroyRetainedClientByResource (retained terminated client) BEFORE liveClientOwningResource -> client.killClient(); server-owned ids (RootWindow/DefaultColormap) correctly fall through to BadValue (hasResource true, no live owner, not retained). Retain tracking: run() finally (X11Connection.kt:92-100) now branches Destroy->removeClientResources (UNCHANGED) vs else->retainClientResources; save-set captured in retainClientResources (X11State.kt:185-192) reading saveSets[owner] BEFORE unregisterEventSink removes it — ordering correct. Retained destroy paths (X11State.kt:199-218) call processSaveSet(savedList,ids) BEFORE removeClientResources so save-set windows reparented out before owner windows destroyed (new processSaveSet(List,Set) overload :2785; old reparents to closest non-owned ancestor — verified by test asserting appWindow reparented to root at x=17,y=18). retainClientResources early-returns on empty resource set (no junk entries). unregisterEventSink discards event selections/grabs/selections/serverGrab per close semantics. Kill-while-grabbed race handled: @Volatile killed flag + processWhenServerGrabAllows (X11State.kt:700-724) gates with owner.isKilled() so a victim blocked behind another client's GrabServer wakes via signalClientKilled (:750-757, clears grab if victim owned it + signalAll) and SKIPS process() of the stale queued request; killClient (X11Connection.kt:4167-4174) closes input/output to unblock the victim read loop -> finally cleanup. XEventSink (XInputController.kt:101-103) gains 3 defaulted methods (ownsResource/isKilled/killClient); X11Connection is sole concrete impl so no fan-out breakage; XCloseDownMode widened private->internal (needed by X11State). Tests: 5 new cases cover length+unknown-id-without-closing-caller, live destroy-mode kill, stale-request-after-kill-behind-GrabServer, retained-by-resource + AllTemporary, and save-set processing; all helpers (killClientRequest:6742, closeClientAndWait, waitForRootChildren, waitForStateContains, toJsonHex) present. Style/conventions match neighbors (named writeError args, request-name dispatch ordering, @Synchronized state mutators). Commit subject matches repo convention ("Handle core <X> requests"). NON-BLOCKING NITS (no action required): (1) liveClientOwningResource (X11State.kt:195-196) does not skip killed-but-not-yet-cleaned sinks, so a 2nd KillClient on the same id during the victim's teardown window re-invokes killClient() (idempotent via runCatching) instead of erroring — harmless race. (2) killClient() closes output without writeLock; benign as the victim is terminating. (3) retainedClients map grows until killed (per-spec retention, not a leak). Gates per brief already green (focused + cleanTest test, git diff --check, Steroid buildAllModules no compile errors).
- REVIEW 2026-06-24: Reviewed current uncommitted core KillClient (opcode 113) implementation (X11Connection.kt, X11State.kt, XInputController.kt, XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt); NO BLOCKERS. Dispatch added at X11Connection.kt:227; killClient() at X11Connection.kt:3281-3294 validates exact 4-byte body first (BadLength/error 16, badValue 0), reads CARD32, treats 0 as AllTemporary (state.destroyTemporaryRetainedClients), then hasResource gate (BadValue/error 2 for unknown/server-owned), then retained-by-resource destroy, then live-owner kill, else BadValue. Error code 2=BadValue and 16=BadLength match codebase convention and the spec's KillClient Value error. Close-down race fix is sound: closeDownClient() (X11Connection.kt:4180-4196) is one-shot via closeDownLock+closeDownHandled, captures mode+resources under lock, routes Destroy->removeClientResources / Retain*->retainClientResources, then unregisterEventSink; victim finally and forced killClient() both funnel here, so live destroy-mode kill is synchronous before the killer's next request (verified by KillClient-before-QueryTree test). Save-set capture happens in retainClientResources BEFORE unregisterEventSink wipes saveSets[owner] (X11State.kt:185-192,806-810), and destroy of retained resources processes the captured save-set before destroying owner windows (X11State.kt:199-218,2785-2802) - spec-correct ordering. Cross-client atomicity of the multi-call killClient sequence holds because dispatch runs under requestProcessingLock (single in-flight request) and each state call is @Synchronized; signalClientKilled + isKilled gating drops a killed client's grab-queued request without dispatch (X11State.kt:700-757). Lock order requestProcessingLock->closeDownLock->{serverGrabLock,state monitor} has no inversion. Killing a live Retain* client correctly re-retains its resources (close-down honors close-down-mode). Interface impact: XEventSink gains 3 default methods (ownsResource/isKilled/killClient) at XInputController.kt:103-105; only X11Connection implements the interface and overrides all three, so the defaults are effectively dead-but-harmless. Tests: 5 new cases cover length/badvalue, live destroy-mode pre-observation, grab-queued-kill drop, retained-by-resource + AllTemporary, and save-set reparent-to-root; all referenced helpers exist. Verification: `./gradlew --no-daemon test --tests '*KillClient*'` BUILD SUCCESSFUL (re-run by reviewer). Commit subject "Handle core KillClient requests" matches the established "Handle core X requests" convention. Commit-ready.
- REVIEW 2026-06-24: Root review of uncommitted core KillClient (opcode 113) implementation found NO BLOCKERS. Dispatch validates body.size==4 (BadLength 16), routes AllTemporary(0)->destroyTemporaryRetainedClients, then hasResource(BadValue 2)->destroyRetainedClientByResource->liveClientOwningResource->kill, returning BadValue for server-owned/unknown ids. Concurrency verified safe: all dispatch runs under the single X11State.requestProcessingLock, so a synchronous victim closeDownClient() from the killer thread cannot race victim ownedResources mutation; closeDownLock+closeDownHandled make close-down one-shot across forced-kill and the victim finally; signalClientKilled wakes a grab-queued killed client which then skips process() via the new isKilled() gate (no stale request applied, killer grab preserved). Save-set semantics correct: retained groups capture save-set before unregisterEventSink, processSaveSet runs once before resource destruction (KillClient and explicit DestroyWindow/free paths), discardRetainedResourceIds drops emptied groups, retained windows stay queryable until destroyed. Lock order requestProcessingLock->monitor/serverGrabLock is consistent; no deadlock. XEventSink default methods keep the sole implementer (X11Connection) the only override; XCloseDownMode widened private->internal (safe). Six new tests cover length/BadValue, live destroy-mode visibility, grab-queued kill, retained-by-resource+AllTemporary, and save-set rescue on both KillClient and DestroyWindow. Verification relied on already-run gates (focused + cleanTest test, git diff --check, MCP Steroid buildAllModules: Compile errors false); no stray debug added. Commit-readiness: stage ONLY the 4 intended files (working tree has many untracked THE_PROMPT_v5*/runs/*/screenshots/scripts that must NOT be committed). Suggested subject "Handle core KillClient requests" matches convention. Non-blocking nits: private fun killClient(body) overloads the override killClient() (valid but slightly confusing); the three separate @Synchronized lookups in killClient(body) are TOCTOU-safe only because of requestProcessingLock serialization (worth a comment).
- REVIEW 2026-06-24 (Review): Final review of uncommitted core KillClient (opcode 113) implementation; NO BLOCKERS; commit-ready as `Handle core KillClient requests`. Dispatch added at X11Connection.kt:227; killClient() at :3281-3293 validates exact 4-byte body (BadLength 16), treats resource 0 as AllTemporary (destroyTemporaryRetainedClients), gates unknown ids via state.hasResource (BadValue 2), tries destroyRetainedClientByResource, else liveClientOwningResource→client.killClient(). One-shot synchronous close-down via closeDownClient() (X11Connection.kt:4172-4189) under closeDownLock; normal disconnect (finally) and forced kill both route through it; victim finally is a no-op after forced kill. Retained-resource semantics in X11State: retainClientResources captures save-set before unregisterEventSink; destroyRetainedClientByResource/destroyTemporaryRetainedClients process save-set then destroy. removeWindow now processes retained save-sets for the destroyed subtree, recomputes the subtree after reparenting, then destroys remainder, covering DestroyWindow(frameWindow). Global resourceOwners map (populated by own()→markResourceOwner, cleared by removeWindow/removeResource/removePicture/removeGlyphSet/removeGlxContext via discardRetainedResourceIds) is the source of truth, so stale per-connection ids cannot mis-target after cross-client destroy + id reuse. Concurrency sound: global requestProcessingLock serializes all request dispatch, so killer's closeDownClient cannot race victim mutations of ownedResources/closeDownMode; processWhenServerGrabAllows skips dispatch for killed clients and signalClientKilled wakes those parked behind a server grab; closeDownLock guards the one-shot. XEventSink default isKilled()/killClient() safe (only X11Connection implements). XCloseDownMode widened private→internal for cross-file use (correct). Commit subject matches `Handle core <Request> requests` convention. Minor non-blocking note: processRetainedSaveSet consumes the whole retained group's save-set on the first DestroyWindow touching any of its windows, so a save-set window under a not-yet-destroyed sibling frame of a multi-frame retained client is reparented out early; harmless (client dead, windows still survive at an ancestor) and not worth blocking. Independent verification: `./gradlew --no-daemon test --tests '*KillClient*' --tests '*DestroyWindow*save*'` BUILD SUCCESSFUL with all 7 new tests present in TEST results XML and passing.

- REVIEW 2026-06-24 08:45 CEST: Reviewed current uncommitted active grab auto-release implementation (`X11State.kt`, `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt`). BLOCKER logged to ISSUES.md. The new visibility helper is correctly called from `unmapWindow` and `configureWindow`, and tests cover active pointer/keyboard unmap plus off-root confine configure. However, `reparentWindow` only checks the final viewability after changing parent/x/y (`X11State.kt:297-303`). X11 `ReparentWindow` performs an automatic `UnmapWindow` first when the window is mapped, and the local spec requires automatic `UngrabPointer`/`UngrabKeyboard` when the active grab event/confine window becomes not viewable. A mapped grabbed/confine window reparented to another mapped parent remains final-viewable in this implementation, so the helper does not release the active grab. Tests do not cover this reparent case. MCP Steroid inspections on changed files reported only existing style/noise categories (`ArrayInDataClass`, `ConstPropertyName`, `UnusedSymbol`); Find Usages confirmed the helper has only the intended three call sites (`reparentWindow`, `unmapWindow`, `configureWindow`). `git diff --check` is clean. No commit message exists; recent history uses short imperative subjects.
- REVIEW 2026-06-24 08:50 CEST: Reviewed current uncommitted active grab auto-release implementation (`X11State.kt`, `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt`). BLOCKERS logged to ISSUES.md; not commit-ready. Confirmed the prior reparent blocker: local spec says mapped `ReparentWindow` automatically performs `UnmapWindow` before reparent/remap, so active pointer/keyboard grabs tied to the reparented event/confine window should release even if the final parent is mapped and the final state remains viewable; `X11State.reparentWindow` only checks final viewability at :297-303. Added second blocker: `releaseActiveGrabsForVisibilityChanges` uses `windowHasVisibleBounds(confineTo)` (:623-624), which delegates to `visibleBounds` and clips by ancestors plus root (:3072-3095). The spec's cleanup condition is viewability or confine window completely outside root boundaries, and the glossary explicitly says viewable does not imply any portion is actually visible; parent-clipped but root-intersecting confine windows should not auto-release. Tests cover explicit unmap and true off-root configure but miss mapped reparent and parent-clipped/root-intersecting confine cases. MCP Steroid inspections on changed files reported only existing broad-file warnings (`ArrayInDataClass`, `ConstPropertyName`, `UnusedSymbol`) away from the new helper/tests; `git diff --check` clean; recent commit style is short imperative and no commit exists yet.

(2) hasGc gate preserves behavior — state.gc(id)=gcs[id]?:XGraphicsContext(id) never inserts, so no unintended default-GC creation either before or after; valid ids still resolve real GC. (3) clearArea ordering correct: window-existence (BadWindow) now precedes InputOnly (BadMatch err8) class check. (4) Test sequence numbering correct: createWindow x2 (seq1,2), clearArea seq3, copyArea seq4, copyPlane seq5, queryPointer reply seq6 — all asserted; QueryPointer reply proves stream resync after 3 errors. - REVIEW 2026-06-25: Reviewed uncommitted patch (ConfigureWindow rejects zero width/height) in X11Connection.kt and XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt. NO BLOCKERS. Spec match confirmed: zero width/height → Value error (error 2, opcode 12), per x11protocol.html requests:ConfigureWindow "must be nonzero (or a Value error results)"; badValue reported as 0 via Kotlin smart-cast of nullable width/height (compiles clean per IDE buildAllModules errors=false). Checks at X11Connection.kt:2127-2128 fire only when the width/height mask bit is set (null == 0 is false), are placed after value parsing but before the InputOnly Match check (2129-2131) and before state.configureWindow mutation (2132) — validation precedes mutation. Value-before-Match ordering is acceptable (X11 spec mandates no priority among Match/Value/Window for ConfigureWindow). Regression at XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt:6235-6263 sends width=0 (seq 2) then height=0 (seq 3) as separate requests, asserts both BadValue/error 2/opcode 12/badValue 0, then GetGeometry (seq 4) confirms original 40x30 unchanged — proves both errors AND no geometry mutation; window is default InputOutput so the InputOnly branch is not exercised. MCP Steroid inspections (all enabled local tools) on the changed regions reported only file-wide weak stylistic warnings (ImplicitThis, InfixCallToOrdinary, RemoveUnnecessaryParentheses) that also apply to pre-existing surrounding lines 2120-2126 — nothing patch-specific, no errors/warnings introduced. Patch matches the established `return writeError(...)` early-return guard idiom (same as d74e65b InputOnly check, same author). git diff --check clean. Pre-existing out-of-scope note (not a blocker): width/height are read via u32 while the protocol field is CARD16, so values like 0x10000 would bypass the == 0 check, but that predates this patch and is outside the zero-rejection intent. No commit to format-check (uncommitted); suggested imperative subject e.g. "Validate ConfigureWindow nonzero dimensions".
