- REVIEW 2026-06-25: Reviewed current uncommitted MapWindow/SubstructureRedirect slice (X11Connection.kt, X11State.kt, XInputController.kt, XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt). NO BLOCKERS. Protocol correctness verified: mapWindow returns early (no MapNotify, window stays unmapped) only when overrideRedirect is false AND another client selected SubstructureRedirect on the parent; mapRequestSinks filters out the requester so a client never redirects its own MapWindow (and since SubstructureRedirect is exclusive per window, the lone-selector-is-requester case correctly maps normally). override-redirect path bypasses the check and maps. MapRequest event serialization correct: type=20, byte[1]=0, seq at 2 (uses the receiving sink's own sequence field via the override on the observer's connection, consistent with all other event senders), parent at 4..7, window at 8..11, remainder zeroed (ByteArray(32)). Exclusivity: canSelectEvents rejects a second client selecting SubstructureRedirect (BadAccess=10, opcode=2, badValue=0); same sink may re-select its own mask (otherSink != sink guard). No regression to selectEvents for non-redirect masks (canSelectEvents degrades to the prior containsKey check). CreateNotify still keyed on SubstructureNotify only, so SubstructureRedirect alone does not select CreateNotify (test guards this implicitly: observer's next read after map is the MapRequest, not a CreateNotify). Stale-selection leak not a concern: removeEventSelections clears selections on window removal, so exclusivity does not persist past destroy. Wiring confirmed via Find Usages: sendMapRequestEvent (interface + sole impl X11Connection + dispatch), mapRequestSinks, canSelectEvents, XMapRequestEvent/XMapRequestDispatch all referenced; X11Connection is the only XEventSink impl so the new interface method breaks no other implementors. Gates reported passed (focused + full + cleanTest, git diff --check, buildAllModules errors=false aborted=false). Non-blocking follow-ups: (1) changeWindowAttributes applies overrideRedirect/doNotPropagateMask/background BEFORE validating the eventMask, so a single request that both changes an attribute and conflicts on SubstructureRedirect partially applies before BadAccess (not atomic per X11 spec); consider validating eventMask before mutating. (2) Test coverage gap: no test exercises the sink==requester branch directly (a client that itself holds SubstructureRedirect on the parent maps its own child and should map normally with no MapRequest to itself) - the redirect test uses distinct owner/observer so that filter branch is not covered. (3) override-redirect test asserts Expose (implies map) but not state.json mapped:true; minor. Maintainer/pattern: adjacent core protocol/state/test code is Eugene Petrenko; recent commit subjects use short imperative form ("Implement selected ... delivery", "Deliver ..."), so a subject like "Redirect MapWindow via SubstructureRedirect" is format-compliant. Acceptable to commit.
- REVIEW 2026-06-25: Reviewed uncommitted core MapWindow SubstructureRedirect patch (`XInputController.kt`, `X11State.kt`, `X11Connection.kt`, `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt`). NO BLOCKERS. Verified against local `docs/spec/x11protocol.html`: MapRequest event spec is `parent, window: WINDOW` reported to the SubstructureRedirect selector on the parent (#events:MapRequest); MapWindow spec (#requests:MapWindow lines 2003-2014) says override-redirect False AND *some other client* selected SubstructureRedirect on parent → MapRequest generated and window stays unmapped, else map + MapNotify; ChangeWindowAttributes spec mandates only one client may select SubstructureRedirect, violation → Access error. Implementation matches: (1) wire layout `sendMapRequestEvent` writes type 20 @0, sequence @2 (observer's own connection sequence, correct per X11), parentId @4, windowId @8, bytes 12..31 zero — matches `assertMapRequest`; (2) `mapWindow` (X11Connection.kt:2096-2102) early-returns before computing mapNotifySinks/mapWindow when redirected, so no map and no MapNotify; (3) `mapRequestSinks` filters `sink != requester` (X11State.kt:456-464) implementing the spec "some other client" clause and the "requester must not get its own redirect" rule; (4) override-redirect true bypasses via `!current.overrideRedirect` guard; (5) duplicate SubstructureRedirect rejected with BadAccess error=10/opcode=2 via `canSelectEvents` (X11State.kt:1138-1144), `otherSink != sink` correctly allows the holder to re-select; (6) CreateNotify still keyed on SubstructureNotify (createNotifySinks unchanged), so SubstructureRedirect-only selector gets no CreateNotify — implicitly guarded by test reading exactly 32 bytes and asserting type 20 (a leaked type-16 CreateNotify would fail assertMapRequest). `write()` is synchronized on writeLock. Only one XEventSink implementor (X11Connection), so interface addition is fully covered; `return writeError(...)` inside the `eventMask?.let` is a Kotlin non-local return from changeWindowAttributes (correct). Non-blocking follow-ups (not blockers): (a) Coverage gap — the explicit spec bullet "requesting client itself must not receive the redirect event for its own MapWindow" is not directly exercised; in both new tests the map requester (owner) holds no SubstructureRedirect, so the `sink != requester` filter is only tested in the negative. Suggest a test where the requester holds SubstructureRedirect on the parent and maps its own child, asserting normal map + no MapRequest to self. (b) Partial side effects — in changeWindowAttributes, overrideRedirect/doNotPropagateMask/background updates are applied before the eventMask BadAccess check (X11Connection.kt:2009-2017); X11 requires failed requests to produce no side effects. Edge-case (combined attrs + failing redirect select); the duplicate-select test sends eventMask alone so it is not exercised. (c) Pre-existing latent data race: `sendMapRequestEvent` reads the observer connection's non-volatile `sequence` from the requester's thread — consistent with existing sendMapNotify/sendCreateNotify cross-thread delivery, not introduced here. (d) Exclusivity modeled only for SubstructureRedirect; spec also mandates Access-error exclusivity for ResizeRedirect and ButtonPress — out of scope for this slice. MCP Steroid/IDE gates and Gradle tests already reported green by the implementation agent; `git diff --check` clean. Commit-ready; stage only the four scoped files (workspace has unrelated untracked orchestration/artifact files). Suggested imperative subject e.g. "Redirect MapWindow to SubstructureRedirect selector".
- REVIEW 2026-06-25: Reviewed current uncommitted MapWindow/SubstructureRedirect patch (X11Connection.kt, X11State.kt, XInputController.kt, XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt); NO BLOCKERS. Protocol correctness OK: sendMapRequestEvent writes byte0=20, seq@2, parent@4, window@8, rest zero (X11Connection.kt:4556-4562) = spec. mapWindow redirect gate (X11Connection.kt:2096-2102) honors override-redirect bypass, sends MapRequest and skips mapping only when a non-requester sink holds parent SubstructureRedirect; mapRequestSinks filters sink!=requester (X11State.kt:456-464) so the requester is never redirected to itself and (given exclusivity) the window maps normally when only the requester selected redirect. Exclusivity+atomicity OK: canSelectEvents (X11State.kt:1137-1144) is same-sink tolerant (re-selection by owner allowed) and ChangeWindowAttributes pre-validates eventMask redirect BEFORE any updateWindowAttributes call (X11Connection.kt:2009-2017), so a rejected request has no partial side effects (BadAccess error 10, opcode 2, badValue 0). CreateNotify isolation OK: createNotifySinks keys off SubstructureNotify only, so redirect-only selection yields no CreateNotify. selectEvents now routes through canSelectEvents (X11State.kt:1148); both callers (createWindow new-window, ChangeWindowAttributes pre-checked) cannot hit the new silent-reject path problematically. Tests verified against helpers (readReply/assertError/assertMapRequest/changeWindowAttributesRawRequest) and correctly assert observer-side event sequence numbers and the atomicity case via firstSocket receiving MapRequest (proving overrideRedirect bit not applied). Concurrency note: mapWindow reads the `current` snapshot then makes separate @Synchronized calls for redirect/notify/map — same TOCTOU window as pre-existing mapNotifySinks+mapWindow design, not a new regression. Non-blocking follow-ups: (1) add a test where the redirecting client itself maps the child (expect normal map, no MapRequest to self) to lock in the filter behavior; (2) the redirected-map test asserts `"mapped":false` as a bare substring on /state.json rather than scoping to the child id — works today (only root+child exist) but is fragile; (3) cosmetic: a destroyed-window race between the existence check and canSelectEvents could surface BadAccess instead of BadWindow. Verification relied on the build/test gates reported as already passed (cleanTest test, targeted MapWindow/SubstructureRedirect/ChangeWindowAttributes filters, git diff --check, IntelliJ buildAllModules).
- REVIEW 2026-06-25: Reviewed uncommitted ConfigureWindow parent-SubstructureRedirect patch (X11Connection.kt, X11State.kt, XInputController.kt, XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt). NO BLOCKERS. Serializer byte layout matches spec and real X11 ConfigureRequest (code=23@0, stack-mode@1, seq@2, parent@4, window@8, sibling@12, x@16, y@18, width@20, height@22, border-width@24, value-mask@26, 28..31 zero from ByteArray init) (X11Connection.kt:4656). Redirect block is correctly placed AFTER all validation (width/height/border/sibling/stack-mode) and returns before state.configureWindow, so redirected requests still error normally yet perform no further processing (X11Connection.kt:2196-2213). override-redirect bypass via `if (!window.overrideRedirect)` is correct. configureRequestSinks mirrors mapRequestSinks exactly: filters requester via identity (`sink != requester`, X11Connection has no equals override) so requester never gets its own ConfigureRequest, and fills unspecified fields from current XWindow geometry while reporting the original request value-mask (X11State.kt:573-602). `sequence` resolves to the recipient connection's field (same established cross-connection pattern as sendMapRequestEvent); test asserts observer seq=2 then 3, confirming exactly one event and correct sequencing. X11Connection is the sole XEventSink impl so the new interface method compiles. Tests are valid: redirected test proves geometry unchanged (getGeometry seq=5) + mask 0x006f exercises border-width-from-current path; override test proves bypass (no event to observer, geometry applied). Non-blocking follow-ups: (1) no direct test for the `sink != requester` filter — i.e. the client that itself holds SubstructureRedirect on the parent should configure normally rather than be redirected; add one. (2) No coverage for a non-root parent (deeper hierarchy) confirming parentId, nor for negative x/y (INT16) which assertConfigureRequest reads via u16le. (3) Multiple-redirect-holder fan-out is unenforced but pre-existing/shared with mapRequest, out of scope. Verification relied on stated gates (focused + full cleanTest test, git diff --check, IntelliJ buildAllModules errors=false).
- REVIEW 2026-06-25: Reviewed uncommitted ConfigureWindow parent-SubstructureRedirect slice (X11Connection.kt, X11State.kt, XInputController.kt, XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt). NO BLOCKERS. Protocol correctness: ConfigureRequest serializer (sendConfigureRequestEvent) matches spec exactly — type=23@0, stack-mode@1, sequence@2..3, parent@4, window@8, sibling@12, x/y@16/18 (put16), width/height/border@20/22/24, value-mask@26 (CARD16), bytes 28..31 zero; redirect block mirrors the established MapRequest pattern (validation/errors emitted BEFORE redirect, then early return with no state.configureWindow and no ConfigureNotify, so geometry is preserved); state.configureRequestSinks reuses eventSelectionsForWindow(parentId, SubstructureRedirect) + `sink != requester` filter (identical to mapRequestSinks), so the requester never gets its own ConfigureRequest and override-redirect bypasses redirect; SubstructureRedirect is single-owner (canSelectEvents enforces BadAccess) so at most one sink; defaulting is correct (x/y/w/h/border ?: current window snapshot, sibling ?: 0, stackMode ?: XStackMode.Above=0); value-mask reports the request mask verbatim. Regression risk: low — new block only fires when !overrideRedirect AND a non-requester selected SubstructureRedirect on parent; otherwise empty list -> unchanged path. Only XEventSink implementer is X11Connection, so the new interface method is fully covered (compile gate green). sendConfigureRequest wraps each dispatch in runCatching, consistent with sendMapRequest. Tests: redirect-preserves-geometry (asserts ConfigureRequest fields incl. sibling/stack-mode/valueMask=0x006f and unchanged getGeometry) and override-redirect-bypass both correct; observer queryPointer seq checks confirm exactly one event and ordering. Verification: git diff inspected; relied on already-green gates (full XCoreDrawingProtocolTest, cleanTest test, git diff --check, buildAllModules errors=false).
- REVIEW 2026-06-25 (non-blocking follow-ups): (1) No test for requester-IS-the-redirect-selector (a WM ConfigureWindow on its own managed child must configure normally, not self-redirect); handled by the `sink != requester` filter but untested. (2) Defaulting of unspecified x/y/width/height from current geometry is not exercised — test 1 specifies all geometry bits; only borderWidth defaulting (bit4 unset) is covered. Add a minimal-mask case (e.g. width-only) asserting current x/y/height reported and partial value-mask echoed. (3) x/y are INT16 (signed) in the event but assertConfigureRequest reads them via u16le; fine for positive coords, but a negative-coordinate redirect test would need signed reads. (4) Minor concurrency note: configureRequestSinks defaults from the `window` snapshot captured outside the @Synchronized block (consistent with mapRequestSinks); freshest geometry could be re-fetched inside the lock. Pre-existing pattern, low risk. (5) Eventual commit should follow the repo imperative style, e.g. "Implement ConfigureRequest redirect for parent SubstructureRedirect".

- REVIEW 2026-06-25: CirculateWindow SubstructureRedirect patch (XInputController/X11State/X11Connection + test). Logic/state/filtering CORRECT: circulateWindowTarget split is non-mutating; handler short-circuits (computes target, if null returns with no CirculateRequest; if redirect sinks present sends CirculateRequest and returns before any restack; else falls through to circulateWindow+CirculateNotify as before). circulateRequestSinks uses eventSelectionsForWindow(parentId, SubstructureRedirect).filter{sink!=requester}, matching configureRequestSinks convention, so requester is excluded. Place value uses Top=0/Bottom=1 = PlaceOnTop/PlaceOnBottom (correct). BLOCKER (serialization): sendCirculateRequestEvent writes place into byte[1]; the X11 wire format for CirculateRequest (event 27) places `place` at byte 16 with byte 1 unused — identical layout to CirculateNotify, which THIS codebase already serializes correctly at byte 16 (assertCirculateNotify checks event[16]). A real WM/Xlib client reads place from byte 16, so it would always see 0 and ignore byte 1. The patch and its test both enshrine the wrong offset. Non-blocking follow-ups: (1) circulateWindow recomputes circulateWindowTarget a second time in the no-redirect path, and the three @Synchronized calls (target/sinks/circulateWindow) are not under one lock => minor TOCTOU + double work, consistent with existing configureWindow pattern; (2) test coverage gaps — no test that the requester (owner) selecting SubstructureRedirect does NOT get its own CirculateRequest (the explicit spec rule), no LowerHighest-with-redirect case, no "redirect selected but no child needs restacking => no CirculateRequest" case.

- REVIEW 2026-06-25: Reviewed uncommitted CirculateWindow SubstructureRedirect patch (XInputController.kt, X11State.kt, X11Connection.kt, XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt). NO BLOCKERS. Protocol correctness verified: serializer sendCirculateRequestEvent (X11Connection.kt:4646-4654) writes type=27[0], byte1=0, seq[2..3], parent[4..7], window[8..11], zero[12..15], place[16], zero[17..31] — matches spec exactly; ByteArray(32) zero-init covers reserved regions. circulateWindow flow (X11Connection.kt:2237-2244) validates length/direction/window as before, computes non-mutating circulateWindowTarget, sends CirculateRequest via circulateRequestSinks(this,target) filtered to sink!=requester, returns before any restack — "no further processing" honored; requester self-redirect excluded. No-restack-needed case returns null target -> no CirculateRequest (spec ok). State split is clean: restackChild moved out of circulateWindowTarget into circulateWindow wrapper (X11State.kt:332-354), restack happens exactly once, no double-restack. circulateRequestSinks (X11State.kt:565-574) mirrors existing configureRequestSinks pattern (SubstructureRedirect on parentId, requester filter). XEventSink interface method added; only implementer X11Connection implements it (compile gate passed). Test asserts redirected CirculateRequest (LowerHighest -> window=second, place=Bottom) AND unchanged stacking order; assertCirculateRequest checks full byte layout incl zero regions and seq=2. Non-blocking follow-ups: (1) non-redirect path computes circulateWindowTarget twice (discarded check + recompute inside circulateWindow) — minor; theoretical TOCTOU between the separate @Synchronized calls, but consistent with existing configureWindow handler. (2) Coverage gaps: no test for redirect-selected-but-no-child-needs-restacking -> no event; no test for requester itself selecting redirect (should circulate normally + emit CirculateNotify, not a self CirculateRequest). Gates passed per handoff (targeted + full cleanTest test, git diff --check, IntelliJ build clean). Acceptable to commit.
- REVIEW 2026-06-25: Reviewed current uncommitted CirculateWindow SubstructureRedirect patch (X11Connection.kt, X11State.kt, XInputController.kt, XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt); NO BLOCKERS. circulateWindow handler (X11Connection.kt:2230-2245) preserves validation order (BadLength/16, BadValue/2 for direction, BadWindow/3) then computes circulateWindowTarget; if circulateRequestSinks (requester-filtered) non-empty it sends CirculateRequest and returns WITHOUT restacking, else falls through to circulateWindow+CirculateNotify (unchanged path). Split of X11State.circulateWindowTarget (non-mutating) vs circulateWindow (target+restack) is behavior-preserving for the no-redirect path. CirculateRequest serializer (X11Connection.kt:4646-4654) correct: type 27, byte1 stays 0, seq at 2..3 (recipient connection sequence), parent 4..7, window 8..11, bytes 12..15 zero (ByteArray init), place byte16, rest zero. place mapping RaiseLowest->Top(0)/LowerHighest->Bottom(1) matches X11 PlaceOnTop/PlaceOnBottom. Requester exclusion via `sink != requester` (reference identity) matches MapRequest/ConfigureRequest pattern; since SubstructureRedirect is exclusive, a requester-held redirect correctly yields empty list -> normal processing. Correctly OMITS an override-redirect bypass: real Xorg CirculateWindow gates redirect only on RedirectSend(pParent)+a window needing restack, with no child override-redirect check (unlike MapWindow), so omission is spec-correct. Only X11Connection implements XEventSink so the new interface method has no other impl to update; build gate confirms compile clean. Non-blocking follow-ups: (1) non-redirect path computes circulateWindowTarget twice (handler + inside circulateWindow) across two separate @Synchronized calls - benign redundant work + harmless TOCTOU, could pass the computed target into a restack-only method; (2) test coverage gaps - only LowerHighest+redirect (place=Bottom) is tested; no RaiseLowest+redirect (place=Top), no "redirect selected but no child needs restacking => no CirculateRequest", no "requester holds redirect => performs normally" cases; (3) commit not yet made - expected message "Implement CirculateWindow SubstructureRedirect handling" to match e.g. 944b76a/36d1d9f.
- REVIEW 2026-06-27: Reviewed current uncommitted MIT-SHM validation-only patch (`XShm.kt`, `X11Connection.kt`, `XShmProtocolTest.kt`). NO BLOCKERS. Opcode constants 3-7 match local `/opt/homebrew/include/X11/extensions/shmproto.h`; body sizes and offsets are correct after subtracting the common 4-byte request header: PutImage 36 with format@25/sendEvent@26/shmseg@28, GetImage 28 with format@16/shmseg@20, CreatePixmap 24 with pid@0/drawable@4/width@8/height@10/depth@12/shmseg@16, AttachFd 8, CreateSegment 12. Fixed-length guards use exact `body.size != ...`, so oversized and undersized requests return BadLength after the dispatcher has consumed the declared frame and stream recovery remains intact. No success path creates SHM segments, pixmaps, or drawing commands; image/shared-pixmap operations end in deterministic BadSeg after the requested basic validation, AttachFd ends in BadAccess, and CreateSegment ends in BadImplementation because fd reply transport is absent. Existing QueryVersion still advertises sharedPixmaps=false, so no visible rendering behavior changes are introduced. Tests cover malformed lengths, invalid simple fields, deterministic terminal errors, sequence recovery, and unsupported-request accounting for handled vs unavailable operations. MCP Steroid inspections on scoped files reported only existing broad style/unused warnings outside the new MIT-SHM logic (`ConstPropertyName`, etc.), `XShmProtocolTest.kt` had zero inspection findings, IDE `buildAllModules` returned `errors=false aborted=false`, and `git diff --check` was clean. Non-blocking notes: PutImage/GetImage intentionally do not yet validate drawable/GC/window extents before BadSeg, matching the task's narrow "simple request fields" scope; CreatePixmap coverage does not include BadDrawable/InputOnly/depth-mismatch cases, but the handler mirrors core CreatePixmap validation before refusing unknown shared backing.

- REVIEW 2026-06-29: Reviewed uncommitted RANDR panning impl (XRandr.kt, X11State.kt, X11Connection.kt, XRandrProtocolTest.kt). NO BLOCKERS. Wire sizes match xorgproto randrproto.h exactly: RRGetPanning req body 4B (X11Connection.kt:7322), reply 36B = payloadUnits=1 with timestamp@8 and 12 zero CARD16/INT16 fields@12-34 (sz_xRRGetPanningReply=36); RRSetPanning req body 32B (X11Connection.kt:7333), reply 32B = payloadUnits=0 with newTimestamp@8 (sz_xRRSetPanningReply=32). Opcodes 28/29 correct (X_RRGetPanning/X_RRSetPanning). Endianness via byteOrder helpers throughout; field parse 8..30 step2 covers all 12 fields. Timestamp uses unsigned compare with t!=0 (CurrentTime) guard, mirrors randrSetCrtcConfig:7188-7191; markRandrPanningSet monotonic, byte-identical logic to markRandrCrtcConfigSet. BadCrtc=178 (FirstError+1), BadLength=16, BadMatch=8 all correct. Rejecting nonzero fields with core BadMatch IS consistent with nearby fixed-CRTC behavior (randrSetCrtcConfig:7210/7213-14 and randrSetCrtcTransform:7274-75 both use error=8 for unsupported-but-well-formed). SetPanning has single timestamp (no configTimestamp) per spec - correct. Test seq 1-10 align; recovery via QueryVersion@seq10 confirmed. Minor (non-blocking): magic literals 16/8 vs named constants (matches codebase convention); assertEquals(true, ...) could be assertTrue (style). Suggested commit subject: "Support RANDR panning".
- REVIEW 2026-06-29: Reviewed uncommitted RANDR panning implementation (`XRandr.kt`, `X11State.kt`, `X11Connection.kt`, `XRandrProtocolTest.kt`). NO BLOCKERS. Opcodes 28/29 added as GetPanning/SetPanning (`XRandr.kt:37-38`). Wire sizes correct vs randrproto.h: GetPanning request body == 4 bytes (crtc only), reply length=1 (36 bytes), timestamp@8, 12×u16 panning fields zeroed @12..34 (`X11Connection.kt:7322-7331`). SetPanning request body == 32 bytes (crtc@0, timestamp@4, 12×u16 @8..30), reply length=0 (32 bytes), status@1, timestamp@8 (`X11Connection.kt:7333-7359`). Bad CRTC → RANDR BadCrtc (178); wrong size → BadLength (16); nonzero panning/tracking/border field → core BadMatch (8) — consistent with fixed-CRTC `SetCrtcConfig` which also uses error 8 for unsupported configs (`X11Connection.kt:7210,7214`). Stale nonzero timestamp → InvalidTime status reply via `writeRandrSetPanningReply`; timestamp==0 (CurrentTime) bypasses the check, matching X11 semantics. Unsigned comparison via `Integer.compareUnsigned` throughout; `markRandrPanningSet()` guarantees monotonic advance (`X11State.kt:6531-6540`). Endianness handled by `byteOrder` abstraction; test covers both LE and BE paths. Connection recovery confirmed structurally (handlers return into the dispatch while-loop; writeError does not close) and behaviorally (test sends RRQueryVersion after the error sequence and asserts seq 10 reply). Minor non-blocking notes: (1) `randrLastPanningTime + 1` can theoretically wrap at 0xFFFFFFFF (X11 timestamps are defined to wrap; acceptable); (2) InvalidTime check and `markRandrPanningSet()` are two separate @Synchronized calls (non-atomic across connections, irrelevant for fixed renderer); (3) InvalidTime is returned before field validation, matching real ProcRRSetPanning short-circuit precedence. Test coverage in XRandrProtocolTest is thorough (bad size, bad crtc, no-op success advancing timestamp, stale-time InvalidTime, nonzero-field BadMatch, post-error recovery) for both byte orders. Commit message should follow repo convention "Support RANDR panning". Root already ran focused Gradle test.

- REVIEW 2026-06-29 (Review): Reviewed uncommitted RANDR panning implementation (`XRandr.kt`, `X11State.kt`, `X11Connection.kt`, `XRandrProtocolTest.kt`). NO BLOCKERS. Wire format verified against randrproto.h: RRGetPanning(28) body=4 bytes (crtc@0); reply `payloadUnits=1` → length=1, 36 bytes = sz_xRRGetPanningReply, status=Success@1, timestamp@8, all 12 panning/track/border u16 fields zero (default ByteArray) ✓. RRSetPanning(29) body=32 bytes (crtc@0,timestamp@4, 12×u16 fields@8..30) ✓; status reply payloadUnits=0 → length=0, newTimestamp@8 ✓. BadCrtc on non-CrtcId, error=16(Length) on wrong body size, error=8(BadMatch) on any nonzero field — consistent with fixed-CRTC neighbors (`randrSetCrtcTransform` rejects non-identity with 8; `randrSetCrtcConfig` uses 8). Timestamp: `markRandrPanningSet()` mirrors `markRandrCrtcConfigSet()` exactly (strictly-advancing, `Integer.compareUnsigned`); InvalidTime stale-check `timestamp != 0 && compareUnsigned(timestamp, last) < 0` matches SetCrtcConfig. Check ordering (BadCrtc → InvalidTime reply → BadMatch field reject) matches real-server precedence (time before geometry) and the in-repo SetCrtcConfig ordering. Endianness all via `byteOrder.*`; big-endian path exercised by `RANDR swaps replies for big endian clients` (GetPanning + SetPanning Success/InvalidTime). Focused test rerun with `--rerun-tasks`: BUILD SUCCESSFUL, all XRandrProtocolTest cases pass. Commit not yet made; history convention is short imperative `Support RANDR <feature>` (e.g. `Support RANDR CRTC transforms`), maintainer Eugene Petrenko — suggest `Support RANDR panning`. Minor non-blocking notes below.
- REVIEW 2026-06-29: Reviewed uncommitted RANDR provider requests (opcodes 33-41) in `XRandr.kt`, `X11Connection.kt`, `XRandrProtocolTest.kt`. NO BLOCKERS. Verified: (1) opcodes 33-41 match xorgproto randr.h (RRGetProviderInfo..RRGetProviderProperty), GetMonitors stays 42; (2) dispatch + operationName both updated; (3) post-header body sizes correct — GetProviderInfo 8, SetProviderOffloadSink/OutputSource 12, ListProviderProperties 4, QueryProviderProperty 8, Configure `<12||(n-12)%4`, Change `<20`+nUnits padded math, Delete 8, GetProviderProperty 24 — all consistent with sibling output-property handlers (X11Connection.kt:6983-7161) and randrproto.h; (4) error precedence faithful: BadLength(16,badValue=0) → value checks(BadValue 2) → BadProvider(FirstError+3=BadRRProvider). ChangeProviderProperty checks mode(body[13]) then format(body[12]) then nUnits length, mirroring randrChangeOutputProperty (7046-7057); GetProviderProperty checks delete(body[20]) then pending(body[21]), mirroring randrGetOutputProperty (7107-7114) as requested. With zero providers every provider id → BadProvider, correct. (5) format/8 safe (format pre-validated in {8,16,32}); Long overflow guarded as in output handler. FINDINGS (non-blocking): (a) TEST GAP — new test only exercises too-short body (err 16) + valid body (BadProvider); the value-validation branches are uncovered: ChangeProviderProperty bad mode/bad format/malformed nUnits, GetProviderProperty bad delete/bad pending, ConfigureProviderProperty non-4-multiple length. Recommend adding cases. (b) CONSISTENCY — randrConfigureProviderProperty does NOT validate pending/range booleans, unlike randrConfigureOutputProperty (7029-7035); this is actually closer to real-server precedence (VERIFY_RR_PROVIDER before RRConfigureProviderProperty) and acceptable, but note the sibling divergence. (c) COMMIT — uncommitted; suggest message "Support RANDR provider requests" per recent convention ("Support RANDR ...").

- REVIEW 2026-06-29 (Review agent): Independently re-reviewed uncommitted RANDR provider requests (opcodes 33-41) in `XRandr.kt`, `X11Connection.kt`, `XRandrProtocolTest.kt`. NO BLOCKERS. (1) Opcodes verified against live xorgproto randr.h: GetProviderInfo=33, SetProviderOffloadSink=34, SetProviderOutputSource=35, ListProviderProperties=36, QueryProviderProperty=37, ConfigureProviderProperty=38, ChangeProviderProperty=39, DeleteProviderProperty=40, GetProviderProperty=41; GetMonitors stays 42 — exact match (note OffloadSink=34/OutputSource=35 ordering is correct per the header, not swapped). (2) Both dispatch (`when`) and `operationName()` updated for all 9. (3) Post-header body sizes verified against randrproto.h sz_* minus the 4-byte common header: GetProviderInfo 12→8, Set*OutputSource/OffloadSink 16→12, ListProviderProperties 8→4, QueryProviderProperty 12→8, ConfigureProviderProperty 16→12 (`<12||(n-12)%4`), ChangeProviderProperty 24→20 (`<20`+nUnits padded math), DeleteProviderProperty 12→8, GetProviderProperty 28→24 — all correct, and field offsets (format@12/mode@13/nUnits@16 for Change; delete@20/pending@21 for Get) match the structs. (4) Error precedence faithful to sibling output-property handlers (X11Connection.kt:6983-7161): BadLength(16,badValue=0) → value checks(BadValue 2) → BadProvider(FirstError+3). - REVIEW 2026-06-30 (Review agent): Read-only review of uncommitted RENDER retained drawable/alpha-map diff in `X11State.kt`, `X11Connection.kt`, `XRenderProtocolTest.kt`. (1) CORRECTNESS: new `XPicture.drawableFramebuffer()` returns `retainedDrawableFramebuffer` ahead of live `windows[id]?.framebuffer ?: pixmaps[id]?.framebuffer`. Retained fb is captured only for pixmap drawables (`state.pixmap(drawable)?.framebuffer`); window pictures keep null retained → live lookup unchanged. Verified pixmap framebuffers are never reassigned in place (no `.framebuffer =` writes in X11State.kt), so retained===live while the pixmap is alive and correctly diverges to the old fb only after FreePixmap+id-reuse — matches X11 storage-refcount semantics and the existing `clipMaskImage` retention pattern. drawableId is never mutated after create, so retained never goes stale for a live picture. (2) `alphaMapPicture` retains the referenced XPicture object (mutable → ongoing alpha edits still reflected; its own retainedDrawableFramebuffer carries the old mask fb after free+reuse). Both writers set alphaMap+alphaMapPicture together (X11Connection.kt:2487 create, X11State.kt:2820 CPAlphaMap); withAlphaMap fallback `alphaMapPicture ?: pictures[alphaMapId]` is defensive and harmless. removePicture/removeResource/discardRetainedResourceIds only touch maps+owner bookkeeping, never the retained fb objects. (3) SCOPE: pure RENDER drawable/picture-state consistency, no new/expanded extension — within `workflow/extension-scope.md` (RENDER row). (4) TESTS: both new tests fail on old behavior — pixmap test asserts 0xffff0000 (old id-reuse would read the empty recreated pixmap); alpha-map test asserts 0xff80007f/0xff0000ff from the retained [0x80,0x00] mask (old id-reuse would read the new [0x00,0xff] mask → 0xff0000ff/0xffff0000). `socket.soTimeout = 5_000` (also added to two pre-existing tests) guards against hangs; windows are created-not-mapped so no events precede the GetImage reply, so plain `readReply` won't desync; new `renderFreePictureRequest` uses correct RENDER minor opcode 7. (5) STYLE: clean and idiomatic; one trivial non-blocker — X11State.kt:4577 `source.drawableId ?: return null` is now a redundant standalone guard since `drawableFramebuffer()` already null-checks drawableId. NO BLOCKERS.

- DECISION 2026-06-29: Re-scoped extension work to essential IntelliJ IDEA and VSCode compatibility instead of broad Xvfb/spec completion. Added `workflow/extension-scope.md` with an allowlist, evidence rule, and parked-extension list. Updated roadmap/test-matrix and role prompts so research/implementation/orchestration agents must require trace or test evidence before adding a new extension or deepening an extension request.

- REVIEW 2026-06-30 (Review agent): Reviewed uncommitted RENDER retained-drawable/alpha-map diff in `X11State.kt`, `X11Connection.kt`, `XRenderProtocolTest.kt`. NO BLOCKERS. (1) Correctness/lifecycle: new `XPicture.retainedDrawableFramebuffer` is captured only for pixmap-backed pictures at CreatePicture (`state.pixmap(drawable)?.framebuffer`, X11Connection.kt:2504) — null for window/gradient/solid pictures, so the new `drawableFramebuffer()` helper (X11State.kt:4885) correctly falls back to live `windows[]/pixmaps[]` lookup and dynamic window resolution is preserved. While a pixmap is alive the retained ref is the same object as `pixmaps[id].framebuffer`, so writes/reads stay coherent; after FreePixmap+id-reuse the picture keeps its original buffer, matching intent. `alphaMapPicture` is resolved at CreatePicture and at CPAlphaMap change (X11State.kt:2821, set to null when alphaMap==0), preferred in `withAlphaMap` (4717) and the alpha picture's own `drawableFramebuffer()` (4721) keeps its retained buffer through FreePicture+id-reuse — consistent because `withAlphaMap` is the sole alpha-map dereference (all other sites only test `alphaMap != 0`). Verified `removeResource`/`removePicture` intentionally do NOT clear these retained refs (note: `discardRetainedResourceIds` is the unrelated client-retained-resources mechanism, not this field). (2) Scope: RENDER-only consistency under FreePixmap/FreePicture id reuse; no new extension advertised; the only new test helper `renderFreePictureRequest` uses already-handled RENDER minor opcode 7 — within `workflow/extension-scope.md`. (3) Tests: both new tests are discriminating — pixmap test expects retained red `0xffff0000` where old behavior would read the new blank pixmap; alpha-map test expects `0xff80007f`/`0xff0000ff` (old M [0x80,0x00]) where old behavior would read reused M2 [0x00,0xff] giving swapped pixels — so each fails on pre-change behavior. `socket.soTimeout=5_000` guards against hangs; no MapWindow so no interleaved events to desync `readReply`. (4) Style/non-blocking: (a) `source.drawableId ?: return null` at X11State.kt:4577 is now a discarded-expression early-return that is redundant with `drawableFramebuffer()`'s own drawableId guard — could be dropped; (b) latent only: adding self-referential `alphaMapPicture: XPicture?` to the `XPicture` data class makes generated equals/hashCode/toString recursive (would StackOverflow on a self/cyclic alpha-map ref) — confirmed currently safe since XPicture is never compared/copied/hashed/set-stored, but fragile; (c) retained buffers/pictures intentionally keep freed resources alive until the referencing picture is freed (expected, bounded by live pictures).

- REVIEW 2026-06-30: Independent read-only review of the uncommitted RENDER retained-resource diff (`X11State.kt`, `X11Connection.kt`, `XRenderProtocolTest.kt`). (1) CORRECTNESS/LIFECYCLE — sound. New `XPicture.drawableFramebuffer()` (X11State.kt:4884) returns `retainedDrawableFramebuffer` ahead of the live `windows[id]/pixmaps[id]` lookup; the retained buffer is captured ONLY for pixmap-backed pictures at CreatePicture (`state.pixmap(drawable)?.framebuffer`, X11Connection.kt:2501), so window/solid/gradient pictures keep it null and still resolve live (window-resize path preserved). Verified pixmap framebuffers are never reassigned in place (no `.framebuffer =` writes in X11State.kt) and `drawableId` is never mutated post-create, so retained===live while the pixmap is alive and correctly diverges to the original buffer only after FreePixmap+id-reuse — matches X11 storage-refcount semantics and mirrors the existing `clipMaskImage` retention pattern. `alphaMapPicture` retains the referenced XPicture (set together with `alphaMap` at both writers: X11Connection.kt:2503 create, X11State.kt:2818 CPAlphaMap, nulled when alphaMap==0); `withAlphaMap` (4716) prefers it with a defensive `?: pictures[alphaMapId]` fallback, and that alpha picture's own retained buffer carries the old mask through FreePicture+id-reuse. `removePicture`/`removeResource`/`discardRetainedResourceIds` only touch maps+owner bookkeeping and intentionally never clear the retained refs. (2) SCOPE — pure RENDER drawable/picture-state consistency, no new/expanded extension; the lone new helper `renderFreePictureRequest` uses already-handled RENDER minor opcode 7 — within `workflow/extension-scope.md` (RENDER row). (3) TESTS — both new tests are discriminating and fail on old behavior: pixmap test asserts retained red `0xffff0000` (old id-reuse would read the blank recreated pixmap); alpha-map test asserts `0xff80007f`/`0xff0000ff` from the retained [0x80,0x00] mask (old id-reuse would read reused mask [0x00,0xff] → swapped `0xff0000ff`/`0xffff0000`). Confirmed `renderComposite(source=PixmapPictureId, destination=PictureId)` direction; `socket.soTimeout=5_000` (also added to two pre-existing tests) guards hangs; windows are created-not-mapped so no events precede the GetImage reply and plain `readReply` won't desync. (4) STYLE — two minor non-blockers: (a) replacing the inline framebuffer lookup left `val drawableId`/`val destinationDrawableId` UNUSED in the destination-only blocks `compositeRectangles` (5605), `addTraps` (6070), `renderColorTriangles` (6121), `renderColorTrapezoids` (6138) — their `?: return false` guard is now redundant with `drawableFramebuffer()`'s own drawableId null-check; only a Kotlin warning (repo has no `allWarningsAsErrors`), cleanup opportunity, where `drawableId` is still passed downstream (6043/6091/6158/glyphs) it remains correct; (b) latent only — adding self-referential `alphaMapPicture: XPicture?` keeps `XPicture` a data class with recursive generated equals/hashCode/toString (would StackOverflow on a cyclic alpha-map ref), confirmed safe today since XPicture is never compared/copied/hashed/set-stored but fragile. Retained buffers/pictures intentionally keep freed resources alive until the referencing picture is freed (expected, bounded by live pictures). NO BLOCKERS

- FACT 2026-06-30: Implemented conservative Expose delivery for successful non-redirect core CirculateWindow restacks. RaiseLowest exposes the raised mapped InputOutput target; LowerHighest exposes overlapping mapped InputOutput siblings uncovered by a lowered InputOutput target, while InputOnly targets, SubstructureRedirect, and no-target paths remain expose-free. Verification passed: focused `*CirculateWindow*`, full `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest`, full Gradle test suite, opt-in IntelliJ smoke, `git diff --check`, and three review-agent reports with no blockers after the InputOnly-target fix. Scope is core/window-manager event behavior for the existing smoke matrix, not a new extension.
- FACT 2026-06-30: Fixed RENDER repeated/transformed drawable-source self-composites to snapshot same-drawable sources before destination writes. The transform/repeat branch now uses the existing `sourcePixelSampler(snapshotDrawableId = destinationDrawableId)` path, preserving transform, repeat, filter, alpha-map, and transparent-miss semantics while preventing live-framebuffer smear. Added a protocol regression where `RepeatPad` self-composites red/green/blue from x=0 to x=1 and must end red/red/green; the old path failed as red/red/red. Verification passed: new focused regression (old fail/new pass), adjacent transformed/repeated RENDER tests, full `XRenderProtocolTest`, full Gradle test suite, `git diff --check`, and three review-agent reports with no blockers. Scope is existing RENDER visible rendering correctness for JVM/IDE paths, not extension expansion.
- FACT 2026-06-30 (Research): Current essential compatibility candidate is `RENDER.AddGlyphsFromPicture` source clipping. Scope classification: essential existing-maintenance inside advertised `RENDER`, because glyph-mask generation is visible text rendering for JVM/JBR/Swing and Electron/VSCode-style clients, and `workflow/extension-scope.md` allows maintaining already-advertised RENDER semantics for visible parity. Evidence still unfixed in HEAD: `src/main/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/X11State.kt:3497-3515` calls `source.sourcePixelSampler()` from `addGlyphsFromPicture`, and that sampler does not test `insidePictureClip`; sibling visible RENDER paths use clip-aware optional samplers when `source.hasPictureClip()`. Current tests at `src/test/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/XRenderProtocolTest.kt:12374-12535` cover basic sampling, component masks, offsets, framing, and recovery, but no source clip/clip-mask case for AddGlyphsFromPicture. Minimal failing regression: create a 2x1 A8 pixmap source with two opaque pixels, set its picture clip rectangles to only `(0,0,1,1)`, call `AddGlyphsFromPicture` for a 2x1 glyph, then composite that glyph with a solid red source onto a blue window; expected output is red then blue, current code would generate an opaque two-pixel glyph and paint red then red. Safest implementation approach: in `addGlyphsFromPicture`, select the same clip-aware source sampler policy used by Composite/Trapezoids/Glyphs (`sourcePixelSamplerOptional` when `alphaMap != 0` or `hasPictureClip()`, falling back to the fast sampler otherwise) and have `glyphMaskFromPicture` treat a null sample as transparent zero. No source/test files were edited by this research pass.

- FACT 2026-06-30 (Research): Current top essential compatibility candidate is existing RENDER maintenance: `RENDER.Scale` same-drawable sources still read the live destination framebuffer instead of a snapshot. Evidence: `X11Connection.renderScale` dispatches minor opcode 9 to `X11State.scale` (`src/main/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/X11Connection.kt:2731-2760`); `X11State.scale` calls `scaledSourcePixelAt(source, ...)` without passing `destinationDrawableId`; drawable sampling in `scaledSourcePixelAt` reads `source.drawableFramebuffer()` directly (`src/main/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/X11State.kt:4572-4637`); `XFramebuffer.compositeGeneratedOptional` samples and writes in row/column order (`src/main/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/XFramebuffer.kt:1484-1505`). Minimal failing regression: create one window-backed picture with red/green/blue pixels at x=0/1/2, call `renderScale(PictureId, PictureId, sourceX=0, destinationX=1, width=2, height=1)`, then assert pixels are red/red/green; current live sampling would smear to red/red/red. Scope classification: essential existing-maintenance inside the allowlisted `RENDER` extension because it affects visible JVM/IDE rendering and is internally consistent with the just-fixed Composite self-source snapshot path. Safest implementation: thread destination drawable id into `scaledSourcePixelAt` and use the same `sourcePixelSamplerOptional`/snapshot pattern as Composite, preserving current color/alpha scaling and source clip behavior. Verified stale candidates already fixed in current source/tests: CirculateRequest `place` byte 16, ChangeWindowAttributes duplicate SubstructureRedirect atomicity, active-grab reparent/confine cleanup, and UngrabKey wildcard releasedCombinations.

- FACT 2026-06-30 (Research recheck): Revalidated current `HEAD` (`8a98a27 Snapshot repeated XRender self sources`) after recent commits. The earlier `RENDER.Scale` same-drawable candidate is now stale/fixed by `8a98a27`; `X11State.scale` and nearby scale tests now cover source picture clip behavior. The remaining current essential gap is still `RENDER.AddGlyphsFromPicture` source clipping: `src/main/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/X11State.kt:3497-3515` still calls `source.sourcePixelSampler()` and `glyphMaskFromPicture` still requires a non-null `(x, y) -> Int`, while sibling RENDER paths select `sourcePixelSamplerOptional` when `source.hasPictureClip()`. Existing AddGlyphsFromPicture tests at `src/test/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/XRenderProtocolTest.kt:12374-12567` cover sampling, ARGB component masks, source offsets, framing, and recovery, but no source clip case. Scope classification remains essential existing-maintenance inside advertised `RENDER`: glyph masks feed visible text rendering for JVM/JBR/Swing and Electron/VSCode-style clients. Minimal failing regression: A8 2x1 source pixmap with both pixels opaque, source picture clip rectangle `(0,0,1,1)`, `AddGlyphsFromPicture` 2x1 glyph, then composite that glyph in red over a blue window; expected red/blue, current code should paint red/red. Safest fix: have `addGlyphsFromPicture` use `sourcePixelSamplerOptional` when `source.alphaMap != 0 || source.hasPictureClip()` and make `glyphMaskFromPicture` treat null samples as transparent zero, otherwise preserve the fast sampler.

- FACT 2026-06-30 (Research correction): Correction to the preceding recheck: `8a98a27` did not include the `RENDER.Scale` same-drawable fix in committed `HEAD`; Git shows the scale snapshot change and its regression as uncommitted worktree modifications in `src/main/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/X11State.kt` and `src/test/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/XRenderProtocolTest.kt`. Because current source/tests now include that in-progress scale fix, `RENDER.Scale` is not the best current unfixed research candidate. `RENDER.AddGlyphsFromPicture` source clipping remains unfixed in both committed HEAD and current worktree and remains the recommended essential low-risk candidate.
- REVIEW 2026-06-30 (Review agent): Reviewed current uncommitted `RENDER Scale` same-drawable snapshot diff in `X11State.kt` and `XRenderProtocolTest.kt`. NO BLOCKERS. Correctness: `scale()` now passes the destination drawable id into private `scaledSourcePixelAt`; drawable sources with matching ids build one `XFramebuffer.snapshot()` before `compositeGeneratedOptional` begins its row/column sample+write loop, and snapshot pixels are sampled through the same repeat/filter helpers as live framebuffers. Non-overlap paths still use live `sourceFramebuffer`, and gradient/solid paths are unchanged. RepeatNone/transformed clipping keeps the existing `scaleClipsBaseDrawable` boundary policy; bilinear/nearest and repeat handling route through `XImagePixels.samplePixelAt`; alpha-map behavior stays consistent with nearby `sourcePixelSampler*` helpers because only the base drawable is snapshotted while `withAlphaMap` continues to resolve the alpha picture normally. The new regression is discriminating: current tree focused test passes, while a temp copy made from `HEAD` plus the new test fails at `XRenderProtocolTest.kt:1007` with expected green `0xff00ff00` but live-smeared red `0xffff0000`. Existing adjacent Scale tests cover transformed RepeatNone misses, bilinear edges, drawable/solid/gradient source clips, and transformed clip rectangles. Scope is maintenance of allowlisted `RENDER` semantics under `workflow/extension-scope.md`, not a new extension. Verification: MCP Steroid inspections on both changed files reported only pre-existing broad warnings outside changed lines, MCP Steroid find-usages found `scaledSourcePixelAt` private with a single caller, MCP Steroid `buildAllModules` returned errors=false aborted=false, `git diff --check` clean, focused regression passed, and full `XRenderProtocolTest` passed. Residual risk: no new Scale-specific alpha-map/self-overlap test was added; risk is low because the snapshot overload uses the same `sampleDrawablePixel(snapshot, ...)` branch already used by neighboring sampler helpers.

NO BLOCKERS. Verified the implementation snapshots only when `source.drawableId == destination.drawableId`, then samples that `XImagePixels` through the existing `samplePixelAt`/`sampleDrawablePixel` paths, so RepeatNone clipping, transform coordinates, bilinear/repeat behavior, source picture clips, alpha-map application, and color/alpha scaling remain aligned with neighboring RENDER samplers. - RESEARCH 2026-06-30: Current next essential low-risk gap candidate: advertised RENDER picture `subwindowMode` is honored for destination clipping only, not for source-window sampling. Evidence: `X11State.effectivePictureClip()` subtracts mapped child rectangles for destination pictures, and tests cover destination fill clipping only (`XRenderProtocolTest` subwindow-mode tests). Current source samplers (`sourcePixelSampler*`/`compositeSourcePixelSamplerOptional`) read the picture drawable framebuffer and picture clip/alpha-map state but never consult mapped children or `subwindowMode`, and no source-side subwindow-mode test exists.
