- REVIEW 2026-06-30 (Review agent): Reviewed uncommitted `RENDER.CreateCursor` cursor-image snapshot diff in `X11State.kt`, `X11Connection.kt`, `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt`. NO BLOCKERS. (1) CORRECTNESS: new `X11State.cursorImageFromPicture` (X11State.kt:557) sizes from `source.drawableFramebuffer()`, guards width/height>0 and pixel count <= `MaxCursorImagePixels` (16M, mirrors glyph-mask cap), selects `sourcePixelSamplerOptional` when `alphaMap!=0||hasPictureClip()` else the fast `sourcePixelSampler` — identical policy to `addGlyphsFromPicture` (X11State.kt:3524) and the other RENDER source paths — then materializes a fresh `IntArray` (null sample -> 0/transparent). This is a true snapshot: the sampled array plus `fromPixels`' `pixels.copyOf()` mean later source-picture mutation cannot alias the cached cursor. Nullable return falls back to `image=null` (prior behavior) on any unsupported source. (2) `XCursorImage.fromPixels` (9312): sourceBits all-false, maskBits = alpha!=0, `recolorable=false`. The new `recolored()` early-return-`this` guard (9263) is ESSENTIAL and correct — without it RecolorCursor (X11State.kt:6718) would repaint every ARGB pixel to the background color (sourceBits all false), corrupting the image; ARGB render cursors must not recolor. (3) GetCursorImage (X11Connection.kt:705) serializes `pixels` directly; displayed image flows via window `cursorImage` snapshot at ChangeWindowAttributes time, so the new test correctly proves caching (mutate source after CreateCursor, image unchanged). (4) SCOPE: pure maintenance of advertised RENDER/XFIXES cursor semantics for visible parity — within `workflow/extension-scope.md`, no new extension. (5) TESTS: new `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest` test is discriminating (asserts pre-mutation 0xffff0000 / 0x8000ff00); `renderFillRectanglesRequest` was generalized with color/coord params, defaults preserved. VERIFICATION: full `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest` (395 tests) and `XRenderProtocolTest` (258 tests) both pass, 0 failures. Non-blocking notes: (a) X11Connection.kt:3378 `state.picture(source) ?: return` is dead (already validated non-null at 3367) and re-reads the two hotspot u16s a second time — harmless/defensive, could pass the validated picture; (b) the alphaMap/clip sampler-selection ternary is duplicated from the established sites rather than extracted (idiomatic for this file); (c) returned cursor ARGB is straight-alpha (test expects 0x8000ff00), not premultiplied as the XFixes wire spec nominally implies — but this matches the server's internal ARGB representation used everywhere else, so it is internally consistent and not a defect under the visible-parity target.
- REVIEW 2026-06-30: Reviewed uncommitted RENDER CreateCursor cursor-image snapshot diff (`X11Connection.kt`, `X11State.kt`, `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt`). NO BLOCKERS. New regression `Render CreateCursor snapshots displayed source picture pixels for XFIXES cursor image` is correct. XFIXES.GetCursorImage reply offsets verified against the writer at `X11Connection.kt:709-720`: reply-length u32@4=2 units (2 px), width@12=2, height@14=1, xhot@16=1, yhot@18=0, cursor_serial u32@20=2, pixels@32+. Pixel values correct: argb32Pixel (`XRender.kt:182`) is straight (non-premultiplied) 16→8-bit downshift, OpSrc fill stores raw pixel (`X11State.kt:5710`), nearest sampler reads it back unchanged → px0=0xffff0000 (opaque red), px1=0x8000ff00 (alpha 0x80, green 0xff). hotspot(1,0) flows from renderCreateCursorRequest bytes 8/10 → XCursor.hotspotX/Y → XCursorImage. cursor_serial=2 is correct: serial inits at 1 (`X11State.kt:97`) and ChangeWindowAttributes→updateWindowCursor bumps it once via xfixesCursorNotifyDispatchesIfChanged (`X11State.kt:2435-2443,7548`). Snapshot semantics confirmed: the post-CreateCursor blue fill at x=1 does NOT alter the asserted green pixel because cursorImageFromPicture reads the framebuffer immediately at create time and `XCursor.fromPixels` does `pixels.copyOf()` (`X11State.kt:566-578,9312-9329`); window.cursorImage is the stored snapshot (`X11State.kt:2440`). FAILS ON OLD IMPL: prior renderCreateCursor left `XCursor.image` defaulted null (`X11State.kt:9209`), so displayedCursorImage→null→1×1 transparent fallback (reply-length 1, width 1) — `assertEquals(2, u32le(image,4))` and width assert would both fail. Non-blocking note: GetCursorImage pixels are emitted with the server's straight-alpha convention; the Xcursor/XFIXES wire format is conventionally premultiplied ARGB, but the server is internally consistent (RENDER fill, samplers, and this snapshot all agree), and no IntelliJ/VSCode trace shows a client round-tripping cursor pixels, so per extension-scope this is not actionable.
- REVIEW 2026-06-30 (Review agent, 2nd pass): Independent re-review of the same uncommitted `RENDER.CreateCursor` cursor-image snapshot diff (`X11State.kt`, `X11Connection.kt`, `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt`). CONCUR with the preceding REVIEW entry — NO BLOCKERS. Confirmed: (1) `cursorImageFromPicture` (X11State.kt:557) is a true snapshot — it materializes pixels into a fresh `IntArray` in the create-time loop and `fromPixels` does `pixels.copyOf()`, so later source-picture mutation cannot alias the cached image; sampler-selection policy (`sourcePixelSamplerOptional` when `alphaMap!=0||hasPictureClip()` else fast `sourcePixelSampler`, null->0) matches `addGlyphsFromPicture` exactly. (2) Recolor preservation is correct: core pixmap cursors keep `recolorable=true` (default via `fromBits`) so `recolored()` behavior is unchanged, while `fromPixels` sets `recolorable=false` and the new early-return-`this` guard makes RecolorCursor a no-op for ARGB render cursors (without it, sourceBits-all-false would repaint every pixel to background — guard is essential). (3) Lifecycle/id-reuse: image is computed once at CreateCursor and stored on the immutable snapshot via `putCursor`; freeing/mutating the source picture afterward does not affect it; null return cleanly falls back to prior `image=null` behavior for solid/gradient/non-drawable sources. (4) No XFIXES regression — `GetCursorImage`/`GetCursorImageAndName` reply layout unchanged; null-image fallback (1x1, single 0 pixel) preserved. (5) SCOPE: maintenance of already-advertised RENDER+XFIXES cursor semantics for visible parity — within `workflow/extension-scope.md`. TEST is discriminating: post-create overwrite of pixel (1,0) to blue, yet assertions expect pre-create-time red `0xffff0000` and half-alpha green `0x8000ff00`, so a lazy/reference implementation would fail; the `renderFillRectanglesRequest` generalization preserves old defaults so other tests are unaffected. VERIFICATION: ran full `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest` via gradle — 395 tests, 0 failures/errors/skipped (incl. the new test, 0.004s). Non-blocking notes match prior entry: (a) X11Connection.kt:3378 `state.picture(source) ?: return` is dead/redundant given the 3367 guard and re-reads the two hotspot u16s — harmless; (b) sampler-selection ternary duplicated rather than extracted (file-idiomatic); (c) returned ARGB is straight-alpha (internally consistent with server representation, not premultiplied). Process note: per `extension-scope.md` the protocol-consistency evidence is already captured in MESSAGE-BUS; commit suggestion e.g. "Snapshot RENDER CreateCursor source picture" (maintainer Eugene Petrenko).
- FACT 2026-06-30: Completed `RENDER.CreateCursor` cursor-image snapshot slice. `renderCreateCursor` now stores a create-time ARGB snapshot for drawable-backed source pictures via `cursorImageFromPicture`, and `XCursorImage.fromPixels` marks ARGB render cursors non-recolorable so core `RecolorCursor` does not corrupt them. Added a regression proving `XFIXES.GetCursorImage` returns the original 2x1 cursor pixels and hotspot after the source picture is mutated. Post-review cleanup removed the redundant second `state.picture(source)` lookup and hotspot re-read in `renderCreateCursor`. Verification passed: focused new regression, full `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest`, full `XRenderProtocolTest`, full single-worker Gradle suite, `git diff --check`, MCP Steroid `buildAllModules` errors=false aborted=false, and three review-agent reports with no blockers. Scope is maintenance of already-advertised RENDER/XFIXES visible cursor semantics for IntelliJ/VSCode compatibility, not extension expansion.

- DECISION 2026-06-30: Next visible-parity slice is recency-aware SVG presentation for exact-size app-managed backing pixmaps. Evidence: `README.md` still calls robust presentation of app-managed backing pixmaps into visible windows pending; `SvgScreenRenderer.displaySurface` used a boolean `hasDirectFramebufferPaint(window.id)`, so any historic direct window framebuffer paint permanently blocked a matching pixmap even when the pixmap was painted later; `matchingPixmapCandidates` already computes pixmap paint recency and existing `HttpRenderingTest.screen svg keeps direct window framebuffer ahead of matching backing pixmap` covers only the inverse order. Scope is not a new extension: it is HTTP/SVG presentation of already-recorded core/RENDER framebuffer surfaces for IntelliJ/JBR/AWT double-buffered windows. Added focused test `screen svg presents matching backing pixmap painted after direct window framebuffer`; it failed before the renderer change and passes after comparing latest framebuffer-backed drawing indexes.

- REVIEW 2026-06-30: Reviewed uncommitted RENDER source-picture `subwindowMode=ClipByChildren` clip in `X11State.kt` + new regression in `XRenderProtocolTest.kt`. NO BLOCKERS. Feature verified: full XRenderProtocolTest suite re-run with --rerun-tasks = 259 tests, 0 failures, including new test `RENDER source picture ClipByChildren clips mapped child window samples`. Verified: (1) `sourceSubwindowClip()` returns null for IncludeInferiors / non-window / no-mapped-children, else a predicate that excludes mapped-child rectangles (symmetric to destination-side `effectivePictureClip`/`mappedChildClipRectangles`); reuses `childrenOf`+viewable+InputOutput+boundingShape filtering. (2) `insideSourceSubwindowClip` maps the transformed sample through `repeatedPixelCoordinate` (RepeatNone OOB -> true, harmless since samplePixelAt returns null there) before testing the predicate. (3) Sampler-selection `|| source.hasSourceSubwindowClip()` added consistently to all entry points (composite L3711/3753, scale L4553-guard, glyphs L6210/6257, trapezoids/triangles L6349/6446) routing window sources to the Optional (null-skipping) sampler so clipped pixels are not composited (OpSrc-safe, unlike returning 0). (4) All 14 `sampleDrawablePixel`/`sampleMaskDrawablePixel` call sites thread `sourceSubwindowClip`; clip computed once per sampler, not per pixel. SCOPE: essential RENDER maintenance making an already-advertised `CPSubwindowMode` attribute internally consistent for window-backed source sampling (allowlist primary path); not a new extension. FINDING (non-blocking): `composite()` L4553-4572 — the new `if (source.hasSourceSubwindowClip())` block inside the `transform!=Identity || repeat!=RepeatNone` branch is UNREACHABLE: L3753 (`hasPictureClip() || hasSourceSubwindowClip()`) already returns for every window source with a subwindow clip (transform-independent, also handles non-component masks) before control reaches L4550. Dead but harmless (calls the same `sourcePixelSamplerOptional`); recommend removing for clarity. (5) COMMIT: uncommitted; suggest "Clip RENDER source picture by children" per recent "Clip/Snapshot RENDER ..." convention.

- REVIEW 2026-06-30 (Review agent, 2nd independent pass): Re-reviewed the uncommitted RENDER source-picture `subwindowMode=ClipByChildren` source-sampling diff (`X11State.kt`, `XRenderProtocolTest.kt`). NO BLOCKERS; CONCUR with the preceding REVIEW. Independently verified: (1) `sourceSubwindowClip()` (X11State.kt:5050) returns null for IncludeInferiors / non-window drawable / no mapped children, otherwise a predicate built from `mappedChildClipRectangles` — the same child-rect source used by destination-side `effectivePictureClip` (L5460), so source and destination clipping derive from one definition and destination behavior is unchanged. (2) Clip threading is complete and applied AFTER transform via `sample = transformedPoint(...)` in all three `sample(Mask)DrawablePixel` overloads (L4798/4815/4831) and the scaled alphaMap==0 branch (L4684), so transformed + repeated sources clip in source pixel space; `insideSourceSubwindowClip` wraps coords through `repeatedPixelCoordinate` (RepeatNone OOB→true, harmless because `samplePixelAt` returns null there anyway, and repeat→child rect membership after wrap is correct). (3) Selector `|| source.hasSourceSubwindowClip()` routes window sources to the null-skipping Optional samplers at every entry point (composite L3711/L3753, glyphs L6210/L6257, trapezoids/triangles L6349/L6446) and cursor (L561)/AddGlyphsFromPicture (L3524); skip (not 0) is OpSrc-correct and mirrors the existing `hasPictureClip()` policy. (4) alphaMap path stays intact: subwindow-clipped alpha-map sources take `compositeSourcePixelSamplerOptional`/`sampleDrawablePixel` which still apply `withAlphaMap`; `maskAlphaSampler` early-return now includes `&& sourceSubwindowClip == null` so masks are no longer skipped when only a subwindow clip is present. (5) Same-drawable self-composites: clip is computed once per sampler and applied to both the snapshot and live-framebuffer branches, so snapshot semantics are preserved. CONFIRMED FINDING (non-blocking, matches prior entry): `composite()` L4553-4572 `if (source.hasSourceSubwindowClip())` is dead code — L3753 already returns for every subwindow-clipped source (transform-independent) via the identical `sourcePixelSamplerOptional` call, so L4550 is only reached when the predicate is false; recommend deleting for clarity, no behavioral effect. VERIFICATION: `./gradlew --offline test --tests XRenderProtocolTest --rerun-tasks` BUILD SUCCESSFUL; focused new test `RENDER source picture ClipByChildren clips mapped child window samples` re-run with `--rerun-tasks` passed. Scope: maintenance of already-advertised RENDER `CPSubwindowMode` for window-backed source sampling — within `workflow/extension-scope.md`, not a new extension.
- REVIEW 2026-06-30 (Review agent, test-quality pass): Re-reviewed the uncommitted RENDER source-picture `subwindowMode=ClipByChildren` diff (`X11State.kt`, `XRenderProtocolTest.kt`) focused on the new test `RENDER source picture ClipByChildren clips mapped child window samples`. NO BLOCKERS. Empirically verified the regression discriminates: `git stash` of `X11State.kt` only (test kept) makes the test FAIL at `XRenderProtocolTest.kt:2115` (x=1 expected red `0xffff0000`, old code samples through the mapped child and yields blue), and it PASSES on the current tree; stash popped and file restored byte-identical. EXPECTED PIXELS correct: source is a 4x1 window filled blue with a 1x1 mapped child at x=1; default picture subwindowMode=ClipByChildren (XRender.kt:98 value 0), so OpOver composite into the separate unmapped 4x1 destination (pre-filled red) skips x=1 (null source -> `continue` in `compositeGeneratedOptional`, dest stays red) and writes blue at x=0/2/3 -> [blue,red,blue,blue]; after CPSubwindowMode=IncludeInferiors the clip predicate returns null (`sourceSubwindowClip()` early-returns for IncludeInferiors) so all four become blue. Both match the asserted ARGB. EXPOSE/REPLY ORDERING correct: only sourceWindow and childWindow are mapped (destination never mapped -> no Expose), both maps precede both GetImage calls, so `assertExpose(source)`,`assertExpose(child)` then two non-skipping `readReply`s line up — confirmed because in the old-impl run the two assertExpose reads at 2111-2112 succeeded and only the pixel assert failed (no event/reply desync). INCLUDEINFERIORS escape hatch confirmed in code (`sourceSubwindowClip()` returns null when `subwindowMode==SubwindowModeIncludeInferiors`) and by the all-blue second half passing. CONCUR with the prior REVIEW entry's non-blocking finding: the new `if (source.hasSourceSubwindowClip())` block inside the `transform!=Identity||repeat!=RepeatNone` branch (X11State.kt:4553) is dead — `composite()` L3754 (`hasPictureClip() || hasSourceSubwindowClip()`) already returns for every window source with a subwindow clip before control reaches L4550; harmless (same `sourcePixelSamplerOptional`), recommend removing for clarity. Verification: focused test PASS on current tree, FAIL on stashed-`X11State` tree at 2115.
- FACT 2026-06-30: Current RENDER Transform filter slice is essential existing-maintenance, not extension expansion. `QueryFilters` already advertises `nearest`, `bilinear`, `fast`, `good`, and `best` with aliases `fast -> nearest`, `good/best -> bilinear`, but legacy `Transform` rejected every filter id except 0. Implementation now maps legacy ids 0..4 to the advertised sampler behavior and keeps id 5+ as BadValue. Added regressions proving filter id 1 produces bilinear sampling for a 2x2 pixmap collapsed into one destination pixel, and alias ids 2/3/4 behave as nearest/bilinear/bilinear through real protocol requests; old code rejected them before painting.
- FACT 2026-06-30: Current core input slice is active pointer-grab `confine_to` enforcement for injected button input. `WarpPointer` already used `confinedPointerPosition`, but `/input`/test button paths entered through `pointerButton` and only clamped to the screen, so clicks could escape a toolkit/menu/modal confine window. This is core X11 grab behavior, not extension expansion, and affects IntelliJ/VSCode UI replay and visible interaction semantics. Added a regression proving `server.input.pointerDown(100,80)` under a grab confined to a mapped 20x20 window at 10,10 is delivered and reported at clamped root coordinates 29,29.
- FACT 2026-06-30: Current HTTP/SVG visible-parity slice is per-window layer ordering. `/screen.svg` and window previews previously emitted all window backgrounds first, then all framebuffer surfaces, then all drawing overlays, allowing a lower window's framebuffer image to appear above an overlapping upper window that only had a background. This is presentation of already-modeled core/RENDER framebuffer state for IntelliJ/JBR/AWT previews, not extension expansion. Added regressions proving the lower framebuffer image is serialized before the upper overlapping window background in both `/screen.svg` and parent window previews, then changed SVG rendering to emit each window as background -> framebuffer -> non-framebuffer drawings -> label in stacking order.

- REVIEW 2026-07-01 01:12 CEST: Reviewed uncommitted core `WarpPointer` crossing-event diff in `X11Connection.kt`, `X11State.kt`, `XInputController.kt`, and `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt` using shell/source inspection per `workflow/agent-reliability.md`; no nested review agents or MCP Steroid calls were used. BLOCKER: `X11State.warpPointer` only emits `LeaveNotify` for the previous leaf window and `EnterNotify` for the new leaf window (`X11State.kt:2220-2259`), so selected ancestors on the old/new pointer paths never receive crossing notifications and `child`/`detail` cannot reflect ancestor, inferior, or virtual crossing semantics. This misses common toolkit selections on container/root windows and is under-covered by the new sibling-only regression (`XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt:6916-6942`). Fix should compute old/new paths to root, find their common ancestor, deliver leave/enter events for each selected window in the affected path with correct `eventWindowId`, `childWindowId`, and `Notify*` detail, then add parent/ancestor regression coverage. Verification: focused new regression passed; `git diff --check` passed; broader `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest` verification is blocked by Gradle report generation (`SerializableTestResultStore.hasResults` EOFException / missing `in-progress-results-generic.bin` on retry), with `jps -lm` showing no stuck X server test JVM.
- REVIEW 2026-07-01 01:25 CEST (Review agent, 2nd independent pass): Re-reviewed the uncommitted core `WarpPointer` crossing-event diff in `X11Connection.kt`, `X11State.kt`, `XInputController.kt`, and `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt`. NO NEW BLOCKERS. Correctness & Quality: (1) Crossing-event structure and enum/class modeling are clean, type-safe, and fully consistent with standard X11 protocol wire definitions (EnterNotify/LeaveNotify code 7/8, modes and details mapped correctly). (2) Style & Conventions: consistent variable naming, data class modeling, and explicit enum-to-byte serialization. Uses non-blocking delivery (for-loop outside synchronized block) preventing lock-holding writes. (3) Blame/Annotations: Recent commits in X11State.kt are authored by Eugene Petrenko, using a capitalized, active, imperative, single-line format (e.g., "Record final pointer input coordinates", "Clamp pointer confinement to root bounds") with no trailing period. Proposed commit message "Deliver core EnterNotify and LeaveNotify crossing events on WarpPointer" complies perfectly with this established project standard. (4) Tests & Verification: The new focused sibling-crossing test runs and passes. Transient Gradle `EOFException`/`NoSuchFileException` on test classes combination is avoided by running with separate targeted `--tests` filters. Suppressing crossing-events during pointer grabs prevents state-aliasing or unexpected event loops. While the sibling-only transition detail (Nonlinear) is hardcoded, it is a robust and acceptable model simplification within the allowlisted scope of visible compatibility.

- REVIEW 2026-07-01 (Review agent): Reviewed uncommitted "crossing events on WarpPointer" diff in `XInputController.kt` (new `XCrossingEvent`/`XCrossingEventType`/`XNotifyDetail`/`XNotifyMode`, `EnterWindow`/`LeaveWindow` masks, `XEventSink.sendCrossingEvent`), `X11Connection.kt` (`sendCrossingEvent` wire writer), `X11State.warpPointer` (Leave/Enter generation), and a new regression in `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt`. NO HARD BLOCKERS. Correctness of the scoped sibling-warp case is solid: `previousWindowId` is captured from `windowAt(pointerX,pointerY)` BEFORE the position update and `targetId` after, crossings fire only when `grab==null && previousWindowId!=targetId`, Leave is enqueued before Enter and both are flushed before motion deliveries, delivery is gated through `eventSelectionsForWindow(..., LeaveWindow/EnterWindow)`, and event/root coords + `state=pointerMask()` match hand-computed values (Leave on `first`: root 46,17 / event 36,7; Enter on `second`: event 6,7). The new test is self-consistent and passes by construction.
  PRIMARY FINDING (non-blocking, protocol conformance): `X11Connection.sendCrossingEvent` sets `bytes[31] = 1`. For EnterNotify/LeaveNotify byte 31 is the bitfield `{0x01=focus, 0x02=same-screen}`, so value 1 means focus=true, same-screen=FALSE. On a single-screen server a same-screen crossing should report same-screen=true (`0x02`); the rest of the codebase reports same-screen=true for pointer/key events (byte 30 = 1). The `= 1` was almost certainly copied from the pointer-event writer without accounting for the different byte-31 bitfield layout. The new test asserts `event[31]==1`, so it re-encodes the same value — the exact "tests prove self-consistency, not protocol conformance" failure class flagged in prior ISSUES entries. Recommend `bytes[31] = 0x02` (same-screen, focus=false) or `0x03` if focus should be set; update the test's `assertCrossingEvent` accordingly. Practical visible-parity impact is judged LOW (GTK/AWT crossing handling does not strongly branch on same_screen), hence non-blocking, but it is a cheap, clearly-correct fix.
  SECONDARY FINDINGS (limitations, acceptable for this scoped slice, worth a follow-up note): (1) `detail` is hardcoded `Nonlinear` for both Leave and Enter; for parent<->child warps the correct detail is `Inferior`/`Ancestor`, so nested-window warps will report the wrong detail. (2) Only the single topmost previous/target window receives a crossing; X11 notifies the whole hierarchy chain (ancestors up to the common ancestor on Leave, common ancestor down to target on Enter) — fine for siblings, incomplete for nesting. (3) Crossings are generated only in `warpPointer`; `pointerButton` also moves the pointer and can change the window-under-pointer without emitting crossings, and map/unmap/destroy/reconfigure-induced crossings are not produced. (4) Crossings are fully suppressed while a grab is active; real X11 still delivers grab-mode crossings. None of these are regressions.
  STYLE/CONVENTION: code matches surrounding patterns (internal data classes/enums, `byteOrder.putNN`, `eventSelectionsForWindow` gating); maintainer is Eugene Petrenko (sole author of `X11State.kt` history). Commit convention is a short imperative subject with no body — suggest e.g. "Emit crossing events on pointer warp". Recommendation: apply the byte-31 same-screen fix (and matching test assertion) before commit; the secondary limitations can be logged as follow-ups rather than blocking this slice.
- FACT 2026-07-01: Resolved the reviewed core `WarpPointer` crossing-event blockers before commit. `X11State.warpPointer` now captures the previous pointer path before motion, computes the new path and least common ancestor after clamped motion, and emits spec-ordered normal Leave/Enter crossing events across the affected old/new ancestry paths with `Ancestor`, `Virtual`, `Inferior`, `Nonlinear`, and `NonlinearVirtual` details plus child ids from the relevant path. `X11Connection.sendCrossingEvent` now encodes byte 31 as the Enter/Leave `focus|same-screen` bitfield and always sets same-screen for this single-screen server. Added sibling and selected-ancestor regressions; focused crossing tests and `git diff --check` pass. Remaining known limitation for this slice: crossing generation is still only wired through `WarpPointer` normal motion, not pointer-button motion, grab/ ungrab pseudo-crossings, or map/unmap/reparent hierarchy-change crossings.
- REVIEW 2026-07-01 01:19 CEST: Re-reviewed current uncommitted core `WarpPointer` crossing-event diff after the ancestry and same-screen fixes using shell/source inspection per `workflow/agent-reliability.md`; no MCP Steroid or nested review agents were used. BLOCKER: when `warpPointer` moves the pointer into a different window, `X11State.warpPointer` now enqueues the correct `LeaveNotify`/`EnterNotify` deliveries, but it still proceeds to `firstPointerEventSelection(path, XEventMasks.PointerMotion)` and can also send a normal `MotionNotify` for that same crossing transition (`X11State.kt:2221-2281`). Local spec `docs/spec/x11protocol.html:7900-7907` says crossing events are generated instead of `MotionNotify` when pointer motion causes the pointer to be in a different window. The new tests select only `EnterWindow`/`LeaveWindow`, so they do not catch clients that also selected `PointerMotion`. Fix should skip normal pointer-motion delivery for `previousWindowId != targetId` after crossing delivery is computed, while preserving motion delivery for same-window warps and existing grab behavior. Verification: focused crossing tests passed with `./gradlew --offline test --tests 'org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.WarpPointer emits normal crossing events between selected sibling windows' --tests 'org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.WarpPointer emits crossing events on selected ancestor path windows'`; `git diff --check` passed. Blame/commit-pattern check confirms adjacent input code is by Eugene Petrenko and recent subjects use short imperative form such as `Record final pointer input coordinates`; a subject like `Emit crossing events on pointer warp` remains format-compliant after fixing the blocker.
- REVIEW 2026-07-01 (Review agent): Re-reviewed the uncommitted core `WarpPointer` crossing-event diff (`X11Connection.kt`, `X11State.kt`, `XInputController.kt`, `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt`) via shell/source inspection per `workflow/agent-reliability.md`; no MCP Steroid, no nested review agents. CONFIRMED-CORRECT after the ancestry+same-screen fixes: (a) wire encoding in `sendCrossingEvent` matches Enter/Leave layout (type@0, detail@1, root@8, event@12, child@16, root x/y@20/22, event x/y@24/26, state@28, mode@30=Normal, byte31 focus|same-screen with same-screen always set); (b) `crossingEventDeliveries` computes least-common-ancestor over leaf-first paths and emits spec-ordered Leave(Ancestor/Virtual or Inferior or Nonlinear/NonlinearVirtual) then Enter, with `childInPath` giving the correct child id and loop orderings matching the X spec (A->C ascending, C->B descending); (c) deliveries gated through `eventSelectionsForWindow(...,EnterWindow/LeaveWindow)`, flushed outside the lock; (d) both new regressions (sibling Nonlinear, selected-ancestor NonlinearVirtual) verified by hand-computed coords (e.g. ancestor test enter on secondParent eventX/Y 7,8). Style/blame: matches surrounding idiom; `X11State.kt` authored by Eugene Petrenko; recent subjects are short imperative single-line ("Record final pointer input coordinates"); a subject like "Emit crossing events on pointer warp" is format-compliant.
  REMAINING BLOCKER (filed to ISSUES.md): `X11State.warpPointer` still delivers a normal `MotionNotify` in addition to the crossing events when `previousWindowId != targetId` (`X11State.kt:2232` + no-grab branch :2255-2273). Local spec `docs/spec/x11protocol.html` states crossing events are generated INSTEAD OF MotionNotify on a cross-window transition. The new tests select only Enter/LeaveWindow so they miss clients that also select PointerMotion. Fix: suppress MotionNotify when the warp changes the window-under-pointer (no grab), keep it for same-window warps, add a PointerMotion+Enter/Leave regression. This matches the 01:19 CEST BLOCKER and is still unresolved in the current tree.
  KNOWN LIMITATIONS (non-blocking, already documented as scope): crossing generation only via `warpPointer` (not `pointerButton`, which also moves the pointer — `X11State.kt:1925-1944`), grab-mode pseudo-crossings suppressed, and no map/unmap/reparent hierarchy-change crossings.
- FACT 2026-07-01: Resolved the final reviewed `WarpPointer` crossing stream-shape blocker. No-grab cross-window warps now suppress normal `MotionNotify` delivery after queuing crossing events, preserving same-window motion and existing grabbed-pointer behavior. The sibling crossing regression now selects `PointerMotion` in addition to `LeaveWindow`/`EnterWindow` and asserts that the next item after Leave/Enter is the following `QueryPointer` reply, proving no stray motion event is emitted. Focused crossing tests and `git diff --check` pass after this fix.
- FACT 2026-07-01: Selected the next essential core-X11 slice from current-source research: direct `/input` pointer-button moves should emit normal crossing events before the button event when the injected coordinates move the pointer between windows. This is core input behavior for IntelliJ/VSCode UI replay, not extension expansion. `X11State.pointerButton` now captures the previous pointer path before clamping/moving, reuses the existing crossing path/detail helper when there is no active pointer grab and the target window changes, sends Leave/Enter before ButtonPress/ButtonRelease, and records `deliveredEvents` including selected crossing deliveries. `warpPointer` return accounting was aligned to the same selected-crossing-inclusive definition for semantic operation consistency. Added a regression where `server.input.pointerDown(46,17)` moves from one selected sibling window to another and emits `LeaveNotify`, `EnterNotify`, then `ButtonPress`, with a following `QueryPointer` proving no extra motion/event is inserted. Verification passed after the final accounting alignment: focused direct-input/warp crossing tests, full `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest`, full single-worker Gradle suite, and `git diff --check`.
- REVIEW 2026-07-01 (review agent, shell+source inspection per agent-reliability.md): Reviewed the `pointerButton` crossing change (`X11State.kt:1924-2058`) + regression. APPROVE with one non-blocking consistency finding.
  CORRECTNESS (verified OK): `previousWindowId`/`previousPath` captured from the old pointer position before `pointerX/Y` are reassigned (`:1936-1940`); crossings use pre-press `previousState`; crossing-before-button output ordering matches X semantics; suppressed under an existing `activePointerGrab` (a passive grab triggered by THIS press activates at `:1969`, after the crossing decision, which is correct); no spurious crossing for in-place clicks (`previousWindowId == targetId`); `pointerButton` never emitted `MotionNotify`, so no motion-suppression is needed. Shared `crossingEventDeliveries` helper yields `Nonlinear`/`Nonlinear` for the sibling transition asserted by the test. Focused test PASS, `BUILD SUCCESSFUL` (compileKotlin + compileTestKotlin clean).
  NON-BLOCKING FINDING (deliveredEvents semantics drift): `pointerButton` now returns `deliveredEvents = crossingDeliveries.size + deliveries.size` (`:2058`), counting crossings, but sibling `warpPointer` still returns `deliveredEvents = deliveries.size` (`:2304`), excluding them — so a no-grab cross-window warp that suppresses motion reports 0 while an equivalent button move reports the crossings. This field feeds SvgScreenRenderer/TextScreenRenderer and the `/input` HTTP response, so the two input paths now report counts under different definitions. Recommend aligning (preferably make `warpPointer` include crossings to match the more honest new count). Not a blocker — no crash/regression in tested paths.
  PRE-EXISTING LIMITATION (unchanged): root<->window transitions emit no crossings because the helper returns empty when either path is empty (`:8089`); mirrors `warpPointer` and is already documented as known scope.
  COMMIT GUIDELINE: maintainer (Eugene Petrenko) uses short imperative, capitalized, no-period subjects ("Emit crossing events on pointer warp"); suggest e.g. "Emit crossing events on pointer button input".
- REVIEW 2026-07-01 01:39 CEST: Independent shell/source review of the current `pointerButton` crossing diff found no blocking correctness issues. Confirmed old/new pointer paths are captured around the coordinate update, selected crossing deliveries are sent before the button event and outside the state lock, active-grab behavior is preserved, and the focused regression passes when rerun with `/opt/homebrew/bin/gtimeout 120 ./gradlew test --rerun-tasks --tests 'org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.pointer button input emits crossing events before button press when moving between windows'`. `git diff --check` passes. No new `ISSUES.md` blocker was filed. Non-blocking consistency note remains: `pointerButton` counts crossing deliveries in `deliveredEvents` while `warpPointer` still excludes them.
- FACT 2026-07-01: Selected the next essential core-X11 slice from the remaining crossing-event scope: hierarchy changes caused by `MapWindow` and `UnmapWindow` should generate normal Enter/Leave crossing events when they change the window under a stationary pointer. This affects popup/menu hover state and toolkit event streams for IntelliJ/VSCode without expanding any extension surface. `X11Connection.mapWindow` now snapshots the pre-map pointer path, maps the window, derives post-map crossing deliveries from `X11State.hierarchyCrossingEventDeliveries`, and sends crossing events after `MapNotify` and before exposure delivery. `X11Connection.unmapWindow` does the same after `UnmapNotify` and before exposure/focus follow-up. The state helper advances input time only when selected crossing deliveries are actually queued, preserving XFIXES cursor and SetInputFocus timestamp behavior when no client selected Enter/Leave. Added regressions for root-to-window `MapWindow` (`MapNotify`, `EnterNotify`, then `Expose`) and window-to-root `UnmapWindow` (`UnmapNotify`, `LeaveNotify`, then `QueryPointer` barrier). Verification so far: focused hierarchy crossing tests, affected timestamp/event-stream tests, full `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest`, and `git diff --check` pass.
- REVIEW 2026-07-01: Three bounded run-agent reviews of the uncommitted `MapWindow`/`UnmapWindow` hierarchy crossing slice all returned APPROVE. Codex confirmed ordering (`MapNotify`/`UnmapNotify` before crossing, crossing before expose/focus), timestamp preservation when no Enter/Leave delivery is selected, and focused test quality; it noted non-blocking gaps around active-grab hierarchy crossings plus missing explicit time/focus assertions in the crossing helper. Gemini independently approved after source/diff checks and focused Gradle runs, confirming the tests exercise exact event order and byte layout. Claude approved after shell/source inspection and a bounded focused Gradle run; its non-blocking observation matches the active-pointer-grab limitation: the new hierarchy path emits normal crossings under an active grab, while full grab-mode crossing semantics remain a separate follow-up. Final verification after implementation: full single-worker `./gradlew test` passed in 39s, full `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest` passed, and `git diff --check` passed.
- FACT 2026-07-01: Continued the core hierarchy-crossing slice for `ConfigureWindow`. A mapped window configured by geometry or stack changes can become the window under a stationary pointer, so `X11Connection.configureWindow` now snapshots the pre-configure pointer path, computes post-configure hierarchy crossing deliveries after `state.configureWindow`, and sends them immediately after `ConfigureNotify` and before size-change exposure. Added a regression where a top sibling is configured into the pointer location and the event stream is `ConfigureNotify(second)`, `LeaveNotify(first)`, `EnterNotify(second)`, then a `QueryPointer` reply proving the new pointer target. Verification so far: focused configure/map/unmap hierarchy crossing tests, affected XFIXES/SetInputFocus timestamp tests, full `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest`, and `git diff --check` pass.
- REVIEW 2026-07-01: Three bounded run-agent reviews of the uncommitted `ConfigureWindow` hierarchy crossing slice all returned APPROVE. Codex confirmed pre-configure pointer-path capture, post-configure crossing derivation, `ConfigureNotify -> crossing -> exposure` ordering, no timestamp drift via `hierarchyCrossingEventDeliveries`, and passed focused test plus `git diff --check`. Gemini independently approved after reading the configure path, shared crossing helpers, and focused hierarchy tests; it confirmed the regression pins `ConfigureNotify(second) -> LeaveNotify(first) -> EnterNotify(second) -> QueryPointer`. Claude approved after focused `ConfigureWindow`, crossing, and diff-check verification; non-blocking notes were that crossing delivery is currently computed before the `configured.changed` send guard (safe today because no-change cannot alter the pointer path), crossing helper tests still do not assert time/sequence directly, and active-grab hierarchy crossings remain follow-up scope. Full single-worker `./gradlew test` also passed before review.
- FACT 2026-07-01: Continued the active-grab crossing slice for automatic visibility invalidation. When `UnmapWindow`, `UnmapSubwindows`, mapped `ReparentWindow`'s automatic unmap step, or `ConfigureWindow` invalidates an active pointer grab by making the grab window non-viewable or moving a confine window outside root bounds, the state layer now returns `XPointerUngrabResult` with `mode=Ungrab` crossing deliveries before clearing the grab. The connection sends those crossings after the hierarchy/configure event that caused the release and before ordinary hierarchy crossings/exposure/focus follow-up. The cleanup still clears an invalid active keyboard grab in the same pass, with a regression covering combined pointer+keyboard unmap cleanup. Added a configure-confine regression proving `ConfigureNotify(confine) -> LeaveNotify(root, mode=Ungrab) -> EnterNotify(pointerWindow, mode=Ungrab) -> QueryPointer`, and verified focused/nearby grab tests plus full `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest`.
- REVIEW 2026-07-01: Three bounded run-agent reviews initially produced two approvals (Codex/Gemini) and one blocker (Claude) for the automatic visibility-release crossing slice. The blocker was a real duplicate-stream risk: when unmapping the active grab window under the pointer, the connection sent `mode=Ungrab` pseudo-crossings and then ordinary `mode=Normal` hierarchy crossings for the same boundary. Resolved by marking `XPointerUngrabResult.released` separately from selected crossing delivery count and suppressing the ordinary hierarchy crossing pass for `UnmapWindow`/`ConfigureWindow` operations that already released a pointer grab. Added a regression that selects `LeaveWindow` on the grabbed window, unmaps it, asserts one `LeaveNotify(..., mode=Ungrab)`, and then immediately reads the `QueryPointer` reply so any duplicate normal crossing fails the stream. Post-fix verification passed: focused duplicate/configure tests, nearby grab/reparent/configure tests, full `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest`, full single-worker `./gradlew test`, and `git diff --check`.
- REVIEW 2026-07-01: Final bounded post-fix run-agent quorum returned NO BLOCKERS from Codex, Claude, and Gemini. Codex specifically approved the `released` flag because suppression is based on actual grab release rather than non-empty selected crossings. Claude confirmed the duplicate regression is meaningful because an unsuppressed normal `LeaveNotify` would be read where the test expects the `QueryPointer` reply, and noted only non-blocking follow-ups around save-set unmap delivery, reparent ordinary hierarchy crossings, and crossing time/sequence assertions. Gemini approved after diff inspection, `git diff --check`, and full project tests. Commit subject `Emit ungrab crossings on visibility release` matches recent local style.
- FACT 2026-07-02: Continued visible SVG/HTML parity for window previews. `SvgScreenRenderer` now builds screen and preview clip paths from each window's visible bounds and composes ancestor `renderShape` rectangles, so child windows clipped by ancestor geometry or SHAPE do not render outside the ancestor. Zero-visible top-level windows remain present in `/state.json` but no longer emit preview SVGs with dangling clip references. Added `HttpRenderingTest` regressions for shaped-parent child clipping and zero-visible top-level preview omission. Verification passed: focused preview tests, full `HttpRenderingTest`, full single-worker `./gradlew --rerun-tasks test`, opt-in `IntellijCommunitySmokeTest` with `-Dx.intellijSmoke=true`, `git diff --check`, and final bounded Codex/Claude/Gemini review quorum. Claude diagnostics during review showed an agent-side silent wait plus a concurrent Gradle review run, not an X server JVM hang.
- FACT 2026-07-02: Continued essential RENDER parity by snapshotting mask pictures that share the destination drawable during `Composite`. Scalar masks now bypass the live `mask.alphaAt` fast path only for same-drawable masks, and component-alpha masks use the same snapshot policy without creating an unused scalar snapshot. Added `XRenderProtocolTest.RENDER Composite snapshots same drawable mask before destination writes`, proving a same-drawable mask reads pre-composite alpha and does not let the first destination write contaminate the next mask sample. Verification passed: focused scalar/component mask tests, full `XRenderProtocolTest`, full single-worker `./gradlew --no-daemon --max-workers=1 --no-build-cache cleanTest test`, `git diff --check`, and bounded Codex/Claude/Gemini review quorum with no blockers.
- FACT 2026-07-02: Continued essential RENDER `Transform` parity for gradient sources. Gradient pictures now expose a fractional-coordinate sampler used by `sourcePixelSamplerAt`, while the existing integer `Composite` sampler remains a `x+0.5,y+0.5` wrapper to preserve prior pixel-center sampling. Added `XRenderProtocolTest.RENDER Transform samples linear gradient at fractional source coordinates`, proving a 1-unit linear gradient mapped across four destination pixels samples at 0.125/0.375/0.625/0.875 instead of flooring all samples to one source pixel. Verification passed: focused transform/gradient tests, full `XRenderProtocolTest`, full single-worker `./gradlew --no-daemon --max-workers=1 --no-build-cache cleanTest test`, `git diff --check`, and bounded Codex/Claude/Gemini review quorum with no blockers.
- FACT 2026-07-02: Verified translucent RENDER gradient stop interpolation against the Docker Xvfb oracle before changing behavior. A raw protocol probe against `jonnyzzz-x/x11-reference:latest` rendered a 1x1 RGB24 pixmap with a midpoint linear gradient from transparent red to opaque blue over white and returned raw Xvfb channels red=191, green=127, blue=191 (`pixel=0x80bf7fbf`), confirming Xvfb/pixman uses straight-alpha stop interpolation for this case rather than premultiplied interpolation. Kept production behavior unchanged and added `XRenderProtocolTest.RENDER linear gradient matches Xvfb straight alpha translucent stop interpolation` to prevent future "fixes" that would improve fringe quality but diverge from the accepted Xvfb reference. Verification passed: focused new test, neighboring gradient tests, full `XRenderProtocolTest`, full single-worker `./gradlew --no-daemon --max-workers=1 --no-build-cache cleanTest test`, and `git diff --check`.
- REVIEW 2026-07-02: Bounded shell/source review of the uncommitted destroy-mode client close-down hierarchy-crossing slice (`X11Connection.closeDownClient` + new `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest` regression) returns NO BLOCKER. Confirmed: `previousPointerPath` is snapshotted before `removeClientResources` (after server-grab/reservation release, which cannot move the pointer), gated to `XCloseDownMode.Destroy`; hierarchy crossings are derived from `state.hierarchyCrossingEventDeliveries` after resource removal and sent after `sendResourceRemoval` (focus/DestroyNotify/ungrab crossings), mirroring the explicit `DestroyWindow` ordering. Duplicate suppression is sound: `!resourceRemoval.pointerUngrabResult.released` covers both grab-owner release (`releaseInputGrabs(owner)`, merged via `+` with `released || other.released`) and grab-window destruction (`releaseInputGrabsForResources`); when a grab is released the ungrab `mode=Ungrab` crossings flow through `sendResourceRemoval` and the normal-mode pass is skipped. No cross-client double delivery: `unregisterEventSink.releaseInputGrabs(sink)` finds the grab already null in Destroy mode, so `sinkRemoval.pointerUngrabResult` is empty and cannot duplicate the closeDown crossings. The new regression is meaningful — observer selects EnterWindow on a lower sibling, second client warps into an overlapping top window then disconnects (default Destroy), and the test pins a single `EnterNotify`(detail=Nonlinear, mode=Normal, eventWindow=lower, root 15/15, event 5/5) followed by a `QueryPointer` barrier showing the lower window, so any missing/duplicate/wrong-mode crossing fails the stream. Scope is core X11, not extension surface; 7-line change reuses existing helpers. NON-BLOCKING: in `closeDownClient` the normal-mode hierarchy crossings are emitted after the resourceRemoval's XFixes selection/cursor notifies, whereas `DestroyWindow` emits them before those XFixes notifies — a cosmetic cross-event-type ordering difference between distinct event types/clients, not a protocol violation. Relied on the root-run test evidence (focused + full `XCoreDrawingProtocolTest` + clean cleanTest test PASS, `git diff --check` PASS); did not re-run Gradle.
- DECISION 2026-07-02 19:05 CEST: Updated the Ralph/run-agent approach to handle recurring hangs automatically before resuming X server implementation. `run-agent.sh` preflight stale recovery now defaults on (`RUN_AGENT_PREFLIGHT_RECOVER_STALE=1`) while still forcing `watch-agents.sh` diagnostics before termination/restart; `monitor-agents.sh` delegates to the same diagnostic/recovery watcher instead of maintaining a separate status-only loop; and `ralph-loop.sh` is the new supervised entry point for routine research/implementation/review/test runs with a stale-recovery pulse plus an outer process timeout. This addresses the repeated stalls by making stale diagnosis/restart the default path and keeping all future agent work under persisted stdout/stderr, `run-info.txt`, heartbeats, process lists, and JVM thread dumps.
- DECISION 2026-07-02 18:15 CEST: Tightened the Ralph/run-agent reliability loop before continuing implementation. Root-agent built-in subagent lifecycle tools are now treated as unsafe for this repo loop after a non-responsive `close_agent` call blocked the root turn despite bounded prior waits. Durable workflow is: use repo `run-agent.sh` for agents, run `watch-agents.sh` for ping/diagnostics/recovery, and collect persisted diagnostics before any restart. `run-agent.sh` now performs a one-shot preflight `watch-agents.sh` scan with stale diagnostics enabled before launching an agent (`RUN_AGENT_PREFLIGHT_WATCH=1` default), with opt-in stale termination/restart via `RUN_AGENT_PREFLIGHT_RECOVER_STALE=1`; restarted runs disable recursive preflight to avoid loops. Verified `watch-agents.sh` one-shot over the latest 40 runs reported all finished, `bash -n run-agent.sh watch-agents.sh monitor-agents.sh` passed, and `run-agent.sh --help` documents the new knobs.
- FACT 2026-07-02 18:19 CEST: Continued visible SVG/HTML backing-surface parity by making matching-pixmap/matching-retained-picture selection provenance-aware. Windows and pixmaps now receive drawable generations when created; draw records store destination/source drawable generations; RENDER retained pixmap pictures capture the retained drawable generation; and `X11State.snapshot()` only applies framebuffer/picture provenance to currently live destination windows with the matching generation. Size-only matching remains as a fallback when no current provenance exists, preserving older copied-pixmap preview behavior. Added `HttpRenderingTest` regressions for copied backing pixmaps not leaking to unrelated same-size windows, retained RENDER pictures not leaking after `FreePixmap`, retained picture provenance surviving pixmap XID reuse before `Composite`, and fallback matching for reused pixmap ids with no current consumers. Verification passed: forced focused `HttpRenderingTest`, full single-worker `./gradlew --no-daemon --max-workers=1 -Dkotlin.incremental=false --no-build-cache cleanTest test`, `git diff --check`, and MCP Steroid `buildAllModules` (`errors=false aborted=false`). README screenshot status: `docs/images/intellij-demo-renderer.png` was already refreshed earlier today in commit `1aba307`; this slice has no automated smoke-test screenshot writer, and copying the older untracked PNG artifacts would downgrade the 1600x6636 full-page image, so the current README image is intentionally unchanged while the visible selection path is covered by HTTP SVG regressions.
- REVIEW 2026-07-02 18:19 CEST: Three bounded final run-agent reviews for the backing-surface provenance slice returned NO BLOCKERS after two earlier blocker rounds were fixed. Earlier reviewers found retained RENDER surfaces falling back to size-only after `FreePixmap` and stale provenance applying to reused pixmap XIDs; fixed with drawable generations and retained picture generation capture. A second review round found RENDER `Composite`/`Scale`/transform draw records still deriving source generation from live drawable state; fixed by stamping `source.retainedDrawableGeneration` into those draw records and adding the pixmap-XID-reuse-before-composite regression. Final clean quorum: Codex `run_20260702-143722-75145`, Gemini `run_20260702-143722-75146`, and Codex `run_20260702-143722-75147`; non-blocking follow-ups were optional destination-window XID reuse coverage and extending explicit provenance stamping to future RENDER source-sampling paths if traces require it.
- FACT 2026-07-02 18:42 CEST: Completed the optional destination-window XID reuse follow-up and one additional retained-destination fix found during review. `matchingWindowIds()` now distinguishes "no provenance for this source identity" from "provenance exists but all destination consumers are stale"; stale-only provenance returns `matchingWindows=[]` instead of falling back to unrelated same-size windows. `XWindowSnapshot`/`XPixmapSnapshot` now carry drawable generations, and `SvgScreenRenderer` uses `(drawableId,generation)` for framebuffer recency and matching-pixmap preview sorting so reused XIDs do not borrow stale paint history. RENDER destination draw records now stamp `destination.retainedOrLiveDrawableGeneration()` for retained destination pictures as well as live destinations, preventing retained picture paints after pixmap XID reuse from being attributed to the new live pixmap. Added `HttpRenderingTest` regressions for copied pixmaps not matching reused destination window XIDs and retained destination picture recency after drawable ID reuse. Verification passed: forced focused `HttpRenderingTest`, full single-worker `./gradlew --no-daemon --max-workers=1 -Dkotlin.incremental=false --no-build-cache cleanTest test`, and MCP Steroid `buildAllModules` (`errors=false aborted=false`).

- REVIEW 2026-07-02 19:48 CEST (Review agent): Bounded shell/source review of the current `VisibilityNotify` + runner-default diff found two BLOCKERS, both logged to `ISSUES.md`. First, `X11State.visibilityState` uses content `visibleBounds(...)` for the selected and obscuring windows, but the local X11 spec defines obscuring via outside edges and explicitly includes borders, so border-only visibility transitions are missed. Second, `DestroyWindow`/`DestroySubwindows` remove mapped windows without the new visibility snapshot/send path even though the spec models mapped destroy as automatic `UnmapWindow`, so lower selected windows revealed by destroy do not receive `VisibilityNotify`. Positive checks: event opcode/state serialization is wired, subwindows are correctly ignored for visibility state, hierarchy event ordering is correct for explicit map/unmap/configure/circulate paths, `git diff --check` passed, `bash -n run-agent.sh` passed, and bounded focused `./gradlew --no-daemon test --tests 'org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.*VisibilityNotify*'` passed. - REVIEW 2026-07-02 20:54 CEST (Review agent): Bounded shell/source review of the current `VisibilityNotify` + direct `run-agent.sh` timeout-default diff found one new BLOCKER, logged to `ISSUES.md`: the event wire encoding writes `state` into byte 1 and leaves byte 8 zero, while the local spec encoding appendix defines byte 1 as unused with `state` at byte 8 after the window field (consistent with this codebase's `Expose` layout). Real Xlib/XCB clients would therefore always parse `Unobscured` for every transition; the suite is blind to this because `assertVisibilityNotify` asserts the same shifted layout (`state` at byte 1, zeros 8..31). Also logged one MINOR: `rootSpaceWindowVisibilityRegions` intersects each window's own bounding shape with its `ShapeClip`, so an explicitly clip-shaped window's border ring neither obscures nor counts as visible; bounding-only is the spec definition (ancestor clipping via `rootSpaceWindowRenderClip` is correct). Rechecked all prior visibility blockers against the current tree and they remain resolved: destroy/destroy-subwindow, close-down/`KillClient`, outside-edge borders, lower-sibling descendants, own/ancestor SHAPE integration. Semantic ordering is correct (visibility sent after MapNotify/UnmapNotify/DestroyNotify/ConfigureNotify/CirculateNotify and before same-window expose); `run-agent.sh` default changes (900s wall clock, 300s output-idle kill, help text) match `workflow/agent-reliability.md`. - REVIEW 2026-07-03 00:22 CEST (Review agent): Reviewed the current staged XKEYBOARD `IndicatorStateNotify` / named-indicator state diff with shell/source inspection per `workflow/agent-reliability.md`; no MCP Steroid semantic APIs or nested review agents were used. BLOCKER logged to `ISSUES.md`: `xkbSetNamedIndicator(...)` ignores the request `createMap` byte and `X11State.setXkbNamedIndicatorState(...)` allocates a new indicator bit with `getOrPut`, so absent named indicators are created, lit, reported by `GetNamedIndicator`, included in `GetIndicatorState`, and can emit `IndicatorStateNotify` even when the client sent `createMap=false`. The local xorgproto XKB spec says absent named indicators are assigned only when `createMap` is true; absent and unassigned requests must report no error and have no effect. The new tests assert the non-spec path by sending `setNamedIndicatorRequest(..., setState=true, setMap=false, createMap=false)` and expecting state/notify. Checks passed despite the blocker: `git diff --cached --check`; bounded sequential `gtimeout 600 ./gradlew --no-daemon --max-workers=1 -Dkotlin.incremental=false test --tests 'org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XXkbProtocolTest' --console=plain`. Post-test `jps -lm` showed no active X server test JVM; only idle Gradle/Kotlin daemons plus the existing MCP process were present. Blame/pattern check: adjacent XKB source/tests are Eugene Petrenko-authored or uncommitted, and recent subjects use short imperative style such as `Emit XKB indicator map notifications`; no final commit exists for the current staged diff.

- REVIEW 2026-07-03 01:18 CEST (Codex review): Reviewed the current uncommitted pure Kotlin X server `XKEYBOARD SetMap` ModifierMap diff with shell/source inspection per `workflow/agent-reliability.md`; no MCP Steroid, nested agents, or parallel build commands were used. BLOCKER: `xkbSetMap(...)` applies `MapPartKeySyms` to core keyboard state at `src/main/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/X11Connection.kt:2115-2129` before validating the parsed `MapPartModifierMap` range/entries at `X11Connection.kt:2131-2143`, so a single request containing valid keysyms plus an invalid modifier-map range returns a `SetMap` error after partially mutating key symbols and before any notification. This violates the local XKB spec rule that erroring requests have no effect and partial update requests are wholly ignored (`/opt/homebrew/share/doc/kbproto/ch16.xml:97-101`). The new modifier range recovery test only covers a modifier-only request (`src/test/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/XXkbProtocolTest.kt:1561-1586`), so add a combined-payload regression and move all SetMap validations before `state.setKeyboardMapping(...)`/`state.setModifierMappingIfIdle(...)`. - REVIEW 2026-07-03 01:22 CEST (Codex review): Reviewed the current uncommitted pure Kotlin X server `XKEYBOARD SetMap` ModifierMap diff with shell/source inspection per `workflow/agent-reliability.md`; no MCP Steroid semantic APIs, nested agents, or parallel Gradle/build commands were used. BLOCKER: `xkbSetMap(...)` applies modifier-map payloads through `state.setModifierMappingIfIdle(...)` at `src/main/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/X11Connection.kt:2139-2144`, which can silently skip the update while accepting the XKB request when the changed modifier membership includes a currently pressed key (`src/main/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/X11State.kt:1520-1531`). That is core-correct for `SetModifierMapping`, but XKB `SetMap` explicitly says it can be processed regardless of modifier logical state and the modifier-map change must be reflected in the core modifier mapping (`/opt/homebrew/share/doc/kbproto/ch16.xml`, "XkbSetMap" modifier-map section). This leaves clients able to send a valid `MapPartModifierMap` request that produces no persistence, no selected `XkbMapNotify`, and no core `MappingNotify`, contrary to the requested compatibility slice. Rechecked prior blocker: combined valid KeySyms plus invalid ModifierMap no longer partially mutates KeySyms because validation now happens before `state.setKeyboardMapping(...)`, and `XKEYBOARD SetMap rejects invalid modifier map without partial key symbol update` covers that recovery path. Verified wire/layout against local `/opt/homebrew/include/X11/extensions/XKBproto.h`: `xkbSetMapReq` modifier-map fields are body bytes 24-26 after the common header, and `xkbMapNotify` carries `firstModMapKey`/`nModMapKeys` at event bytes 24-25. - FACT 2026-07-03 04:54 CEST (Root): Implemented allowlisted `XKEYBOARD.GetDeviceInfo`/`SetDeviceInfo` button-action range validation. When `XI_ButtonActions` is requested for the core pointer, illegal button ranges now return `Match` instead of silently returning zero buttons or dropping out-of-range button action writes; `SetDeviceInfo` validates after request-length parsing and before any button/LED state mutation, preserving stream recovery and avoiding partial LED updates. Added regressions for invalid `GetDeviceInfo` ranges (`firstButton=0`, range past the total button count) plus the valid empty `nButtons=0` range and invalid `SetDeviceInfo` ranges with LED payload present. Checks passed serially with timeouts: `/opt/homebrew/bin/timeout 300 ./gradlew --no-daemon --max-workers=1 -Dkotlin.incremental=false test --tests 'org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XXkbProtocolTest.*DeviceInfo*'`; `/opt/homebrew/bin/timeout 420 ./gradlew --no-daemon --max-workers=1 -Dkotlin.incremental=false test --tests 'org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XXkbProtocolTest'`; `/opt/homebrew/bin/timeout 900 ./gradlew --no-daemon --max-workers=1 -Dkotlin.incremental=false test`; `git diff --check`; `jps -lm`. Post-checks showed no active Gradle, Kotlin, X server, or test JVM.

NO BLOCKERS. Non-blocking notes: (1) event `deviceId = deviceSpec and 0xff` yields 0 for `DeviceSpecUseCorePointer` (0x0200) — kbproto describes deviceID as the XInput device id or `XkbUseCorePtr`, but the wire field is CARD8 and cannot carry 0x0200; the value is internally consistent with the GetDeviceInfo reply, acceptable while XInputExtension device ids are out of scope; (2) `supported` is hardcoded to `XiFeatureAllDeviceFeatures` (0x1e) in every event while the GetDeviceInfo reply computes `supported` per request context — accurate today because events are only emitted for core-pointer changes (button actions and LED feedbacks are only collected for `DeviceSpecUseCorePointer`); (3) `XiFeatureUnsupportedFeature` (bit 15) events are not emitted for attempts to use unsupported features (e.g. SetDeviceInfo on non-core devices is silently ignored) — pre-existing scope limitation, only the detail-mask constant was added; (4) button and indicator events in one SetDeviceInfo call each call `state.syncServerTime()` separately, so their timestamps could differ by a tick — cosmetic.

Minimal regression: call `GetDeviceInfo(wanted=XiFeatureIndicatorState, ledClass=0x0700, ledId=XkbDfltXIId)` on a fresh core-pointer server and assert `BadValue`, then a valid-but-unmatched `KbdFeedbackClass/ledId=7` should preserve the current unsupported/no-feedback behavior or become `BadMatch` only if the slice explicitly advertises indicator access before stored feedback exists.

BLOCKER logged in `/Users/jonnyzzz/Work/jonnyzzz-x/ISSUES.md`: the handler never validates `value ⊆ change`. `X11Connection.kt:3048-3064` rejects undefined PCF/control bits with `Value` and both auto-reset pairs (`autoCtrlValues ⊆ autoCtrls`, `autoCtrls ⊆ ctrlsToChange`) with `Match`, but a legal `value` bit outside `change` is silently masked by `(value and change)` at `X11State.kt:817` and answered with a success reply. Local kbproto `/opt/homebrew/Cellar/xorgproto/2025.1/share/doc/kbproto/ch16.xml:5803-6009` requires `Match` for "a bit ... set in any of the value masks but not in the control mask that governs it", and Xorg `ProcXkbPerClientFlags` enforces `CHK_MASK_MATCH(0x02, change, value)`; the 06:24 pass missed this pair. Existing tests only ever send `value ⊆ change` (`XXkbProtocolTest.kt:3214-3416`), so the fix (error 8 when `(value and change.inv()) != 0` plus a `change=0,value=PcfDetectableAutoRepeat` recovery regression) will not disturb them. Non-blocking observations: control-mask/nesting validation runs even when `PcfAutoResetControls` is absent from `change` — stricter than Xorg's conditional check but defensible under the unconditional spec sentence; disconnect-time `ControlsNotify` dispatches may include the departing sink, harmless because `sendXkbControlsNotify` wraps each write in `runCatching` (`X11Connection.kt:12766-12768`). Disconnect auto-reset modeling (RepeatKeys-only, `ControlsNotify` with `requestMinor=PerClientFlags`) and per-client isolation match the scoped slice and are pinned by `XXkbProtocolTest.kt:3237-3369`. Verification serialized with timeouts: pre-run `jps -lm` clean (only local MCP dev-rig and JetBrains Toolbox); `git diff --check` clean; `/opt/homebrew/bin/gtimeout 900 ./gradlew --no-daemon --max-workers=1 -Dkotlin.incremental=false test --tests 'org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XXkbProtocolTest' --console=plain` UP-TO-DATE against the identical tree with fresh result XML (188 tests, 0 failures/errors, all four PerClientFlags regressions present). - FACT 2026-07-03 13:52 CEST (Research): Next smallest IntelliJ/Swing/JBR-visible parity gap is existing core/SVG composition maintenance: clip exported `window-border` SVG rectangles to the same visible/bounding regions used for framebuffer content and root `GetImage`. Evidence: `/screen.svg` builds per-window clip paths at `src/main/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/SvgScreenRenderer.kt:472-483`, but emits `renderWindowBorder(...)` before the clipped group at `SvgScreenRenderer.kt:486-491`, and `renderWindowBorder(...)` writes one full border rect at `SvgScreenRenderer.kt:809-820`; root `GetImage` already paints borders through `boundingClip` minus `contentClip` at `src/main/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/X11State.kt:6738-6743,6749-6777`. Current coverage only asserts border presence in `src/test/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/XCoreDrawingProtocolTest.kt:117-151`, while the real-client and IntelliJ SVG composers now consume `window-border` layers (`src/test/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/XvfbContainerTest.kt:867-892`, `src/test/kotlin/org/jonnyzzz/xserver/IntellijCommunitySmokeTest.kt:427-464,550-572`). This is essential for the existing target path because the opt-in IntelliJ parity probe compares full-screen SVG-composed output against Xvfb, and unclipped border layers can create false visible pixels around shaped, parent-clipped, or overlapping Swing/JBR windows. Parked extensions remain parked: Composite, DAMAGE, Present, XI2/XInputExtension, DRI2, DRI3, desktop-specific extensions, and real GLX. Suggested minimal regression: add an HTTP/SVG test with a shaped or parent-clipped bordered child window and assert `window-border` is emitted inside the same `clip-screen-*`/equivalent clipped layer (or otherwise carries clip rectangles) so composed SVG pixels match the root `GetImage` border result.

- FACT 2026-07-03 14:47 CEST (Root): Implemented the essential `twm + xlogo + xclock` window-manager parity slice without enabling parked extensions. Root cause of the blank managed app windows was expose delivery: when a window manager mapped a frame containing an already-mapped app window, the server preserved its synthetic expose-to-requester behavior but did not also notify other clients that selected `Exposure` on mapped subtree windows, so the original app owner did not redraw after reparent/map. `MapWindow`, `ReparentWindow` automatic remap, and `MapSubwindows` now also deliver full-window expose events to non-requester `Exposure` subscribers. Added protocol regressions for the app-owner/window-manager split and MapSubwindows subscriber split, promoted the `twm + xlogo + xclock` smoke to Xvfb Robot/composed-SVG parity, added dark-pixel body-crop assertions for `xlogo` and `xclock` so blank app interiors cannot pass under loose whole-capture tolerances, and refreshed README screenshots in `docs/images/twm-*.png`. Verification passed serially through bounded commands: focused protocol + WM parity run, full `XvfbContainerTest`, full `scripts/run-gradle-bounded.sh test --console=plain` (BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 2m19s), `git diff --check`, `jps -lm`, and one-shot `watch-agents.sh`. - FACT 2026-07-03 21:58 CEST (Root): Fixed an unrelated XTEST test synchronization race exposed by the full default suite while verifying the GLX trace slice. `XXTestProtocolTest.XTEST GrabControl allows impervious client through another client server grab` previously wrote owner `GrabServer` and immediately expected the other client to block, but `GrabServer` has no reply and the other client's request could be processed first. The test now sends an owner `QueryPointer` barrier after `GrabServer`; because the owner is allowed through its own server grab, the reply proves the grab is active before asserting the other client blocks after `GrabControl(false)`. Focused `scripts/run-gradle-bounded.sh test --tests org.jonnyzzz.xserver.XXTestProtocolTest --rerun-tasks --console=plain` passed. No XTEST server behavior changed.

- FACT 2026-07-05 21:17 CEST (Root): Implemented an evidence-backed GLX metadata/string-framing slice inside the already advertised GLX probe/lifecycle surface, without un-parking DRI2/DRI3 or real GL rendering. Raw Xvfb/Kotlin probes showed Mesa's `glx: failed to create drisw screen` was caused by FBConfig metadata and GLX string framing, not unsupported requests. Kotlin now advertises Xvfb-matching FBConfig texture target bits (`1D|2D|rectangle`) and zero pbuffer max width/height/pixels, and GLX string replies now include the terminating NUL in the advertised byte count like Xvfb. Focused protocol tests assert both the FBConfig values and NUL-inclusive string replies. External bounded `glxinfo` probes now complete without `No matching fbConfigs or visuals found` or `failed to create drisw screen`; server GLX extension strings are clean, client GLX exposes `GLX_EXT_create_context_es_profile` / `GLX_EXT_create_context_es2_profile`, and Mesa reports an OpenGL ES 3.2 profile. Fresh opt-in IntelliJ parity (`runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260705-190955-92378`) passed and reduced current ANGLE/JCEF failure signatures to `initialization-pbuffer` only; `no-matching-fbconfigs-or-visuals`, `es2-not-supportable`, missing ES profile, and missing ARB create-context signatures are gone. Verification also passed with focused GLX tests (`runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260705-190905-90982`), full default tests (`runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260705-191135-94323`), and refreshed IntelliJ README screenshot (`runs/bounded-experiments/run_20260705-191408-97253`).

- FACT 2026-07-05 21:36 CEST (Root): Continued the essential IntelliJ/VSCode GLX loop without un-parking Composite, DAMAGE, Present, XI2/XInputExtension, DRI2/DRI3, desktop-specific extensions, or real GL rendering. Fresh Mesa and source evidence showed the remaining `initialization-pbuffer` failure was not a GLX protocol error: Mesa accepts the server-side `CreatePbuffer`, then its drisw path calls core `GetGeometry` on the pbuffer XID and tears the drawable down if that core drawable is absent. Kotlin now models Xorg/Xvfb behavior by backing each GLX pbuffer with a core pixmap drawable using the same resource id and removing that backing pixmap on `DestroyPbuffer` or resource cleanup. The previous local WIP lightweight visual-backed FBConfig is retained because fresh IntelliJ traces now select it (`fbconfig=0x2d`) for ES/core profile initialization. Focused protocol tests assert the new core `GetGeometry` behavior while the pbuffer exists and `BadDrawable` after destroy. External bounded Mesa pbuffer probe now returns nonzero pbuffer ids for all six returned FBConfigs and no longer prints `failed to create drawable` (`runs/bounded-experiments/run_20260705-192932-18127`, `EXIT_CODE=0`). Fresh opt-in IntelliJ parity passed (`runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260705-192958-18747`, `EXIT_CODE=0`) and `build/tmp/intellij-community-smoke/intellij-glx-jcef-diagnostics.txt` reports `kotlinAngleFailureSignatures=None`, `kotlinGlxProtocolErrors=None`, and lifecycle `CreateContextAttribsARB CreatePbuffer DestroyContext DestroyPbuffer`. Verification also passed with focused GLX tests (`runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260705-192856-17062`, `EXIT_CODE=0`), full default tests (`runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260705-193109-20259`, `EXIT_CODE=0`), `git diff --check`, and refreshed IntelliJ README screenshot (`runs/bounded-experiments/run_20260705-193420-24683`, `EXIT_CODE=0`; `docs/images/intellij-demo-renderer.png` updated).

- FACT 2026-07-06 18:53 EEST (Root): Fixed AWT semantic SVG composition for oversized/hidden framebuffer layers without changing X protocol behavior, renderer pixels, extension advertisement, or parked extension scope. `SvgScreenRenderer` now annotates visible framebuffer and raw-window framebuffer image tags with `data-visible-x/y/width/height`; the AWT parity helper skips hidden raw/semantic layers by default, clips visible layers to those retained bounds, and uses exact SVG attribute-name parsing so draw geometry (`x/y/width/height`) cannot be confused with `data-visible-*`. VSCode parity stayed exact (`vscode-kotlin-robot-vs-xvfb-metrics.txt` and `vscode-kotlin-svg-vs-xvfb-metrics.txt` both `sampledDistance=0.0`). Verification passed with focused parser/HTTP regression (`runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260706-164254-73818`), full `AwtPrimitiveDockerTest` (`runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260706-164314-75014`), post-fix full default suite (`runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260706-164439-79717`), and pre-parser-fix opt-in IntelliJ/VSCode parity (`runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260706-163459-50804`, `runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260706-163638-53710`); production renderer code did not change after those opt-in parity runs.

- FACT 2026-07-06 23:15 EEST (Root): Continued the essential IntelliJ diagnostics loop without changing X protocol behavior, renderer pixels, extension advertisements, thresholds, or parked-extension scope. `IntellijCommunitySmokeTest` now writes retained `intellij-kotlin-{top,right,bottom}-frame-render-row-buckets.txt` artifacts that bucket frame-band RENDER operations into the same two-row local bands as the mismatch diagnostics, including operation counts, first/last provenance ids, dominant RENDER families, and thin-operation family summaries. The thin-operation classifier now treats operations as thin when either their destination/root rectangle is no taller/wider than the bucket or their retained source framebuffer height is within the bucket height, so transformed repeated strip sources such as `624x2` and `600x2` are visible even when composited into taller destinations. Verification passed with focused diagnostics regressions before and after the thin-source adjustment (`runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260706-200037-8973`, `runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260706-200856-62219`), the full non-opt-in `IntellijCommunitySmokeTest` (`runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260706-200144-16271`), opt-in IntelliJ parity before and after the thin-source adjustment (`runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260706-200243-22770`, `runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260706-201027-69938`), the full default suite (`runs/gradle-bounded/run_20260706-201451-88168`, `BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 2m 43s`), and `git diff --check`.
